    10→date: '2025-12-28'
    11→---
    12→
    13→# Architecture Decision Document - MMS Metro Mileage Management System
    14→
    15→_This document defines the technical architecture for the MMS railway fleet management demo system._
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→## 1. System Overview
    20→
    21→### 1.1 Architecture Vision
    22→
    23→MMS is a **demo-focused system** designed to win a competitive bid through a 15-minute presentation. The architecture prioritizes:
    24→
    25→1. **Demo Stability** - 15 minutes without crashes
    26→2. **Visual Impact** - Fast rendering, smooth interactions
    27→3. **Domain Credibility** - Correct railway terminology, realistic behavior
    28→4. **Technical Proof** - Working solver, real-time recommendations
    29→
    30→### 1.2 High-Level Architecture
    31→
    32→```
    33→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    34→│                         BROWSER                                  │
    35→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
    36→│  │                    Next.js 14 App                        │    │
    37→│  │  ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐    │    │
    38→│  │  │Dashboard │ │   Map    │ │Allocation│ │ Analysis │    │    │
    39→│  │  │  Page    │ │Component │ │  Page    │ │   Page   │    │    │
    40→│  │  └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘    │    │
    41→│  │                      ↓ HTTP/REST                         │    │
    42→│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
    43→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    44→                              │
    45→                              ▼
    46→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    47→│                         SERVER                                   │
    48→│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
    49→│  │                  Fastify API Server                      │    │
    50→│  │  ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐    │    │
    51→│  │  │Dashboard │ │  Trains  │ │Allocation│ │Simulation│    │    │
    52→│  │  │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │    │    │
    53→│  │  └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘    │    │
    54→│  │                      ↓                                   │    │
    55→│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │    │
    56→│  │  │              Service Layer                       │    │    │
    57→│  │  │  TrainService | AllocationService | SolverClient│    │    │
    58→│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │    │
    59→│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
    60→│                    ↓                           ↓                 │
    61→│  ┌─────────────────────┐       ┌─────────────────────────┐      │
    62→│  │    PostgreSQL 16    │       │   Python Solver Service │      │
    63→│  │  ┌───────────────┐  │       │  ┌───────────────────┐  │      │
    64→│  │  │    trains     │  │       │  │  OR-Tools CP-SAT  │  │      │
    65→│  │  │    depots     │  │       │  │  Recommendation   │  │      │
    66→│  │  │   services    │  │       │  │     Engine        │  │      │
    67→│  │  │  allocations  │  │       │  └───────────────────┘  │      │
    68→│  │  └───────────────┘  │       │         ↑ HTTP          │      │
    69→│  └─────────────────────┘       └─────────────────────────┘      │
    70→│            ↑                                                     │
    71→│  ┌─────────────────────┐                                        │
    72→│  │       Redis         │ (session cache, solver results)        │
    73→│  └─────────────────────┘                                        │
    74→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    75→```
    76→
    77→### 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    78→
    79→| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    80→|----------|--------|-----------|
    81→| Frontend Framework | Next.js 14 (App Router) | SSR for initial load, React ecosystem |
    82→| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid development, consistent design |
    83→| Backend Framework | Fastify | Performance, TypeScript support |
    84→| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | Reliable, JSON support, demo-proven |
    85→| Solver | Python + OR-Tools | Best-in-class optimization, justifications |
    86→| State Management | @zazcart/toolkit | Avoids React hook pitfalls |
    87→| API Style | REST | Simplicity for demo scope |
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→## 2. Component Architecture
    92→
    93→### 2.1 Frontend Structure
    94→
    95→```
    96→src/
    97→├── app/                      # Next.js App Router
    98→│   ├── layout.tsx            # Root layout with nav
    99→│   ├── page.tsx              # Dashboard (home)
   100→│   ├── fleet/
   101→│   │   ├── page.tsx          # Train list
   102→│   │   └── [id]/page.tsx     # Train details
   103→│   ├── allocation/
   104→│   │   └── page.tsx          # Service allocation
   105→│   ├── analysis/
   106→│   │   └── page.tsx          # Km balancing, simulation
   107→│   └── schedule/
   108→│       └── page.tsx          # Maintenance Gantt
   109→│
   110→├── components/
   111→│   ├── ui/                   # shadcn/ui components
   112→│   ├── dashboard/
   113→│   │   ├── KPICard.tsx
   114→│   │   ├── KPIGrid.tsx
   115→│   │   └── DepotCapacity.tsx
   116→│   ├── map/
   117→│   │   ├── FleetMap.tsx
   118→│   │   ├── TrainMarker.tsx
   119→│   │   └── DepotMarker.tsx
   120→│   ├── allocation/
   121→│   │   ├── ServiceList.tsx
   122→│   │   ├── RecommendationCard.tsx
   123→│   │   └── JustificationPanel.tsx
   124→│   ├── analysis/
   125→│   │   ├── DistributionChart.tsx
   126→│   │   ├── ScenarioComparison.tsx
   127→│   │   └── TrainRanking.tsx
   128→│   └── gantt/
   129→│       ├── MaintenanceGantt.tsx
   130→│       └── GanttBlock.tsx
   131→│
   132→├── lib/
   133→│   ├── api.ts                # API client
   134→│   ├── types.ts              # TypeScript interfaces
   135→│   └── utils.ts              # Utilities
   136→│
   137→└── stores/                   # @zazcart/toolkit state
   138→    ├── fleetStore.ts
   139→    ├── allocationStore.ts
   140→    └── simulationStore.ts
   141→```
   142→
   143→### 2.2 Backend Structure
   144→
   145→```
   146→server/
   147→├── src/
   148→│   ├── index.ts              # Fastify server entry
   149→│   ├── routes/
   150→│   │   ├── dashboard.ts      # GET /api/dashboard/*
   151→│   │   ├── trains.ts         # GET/POST /api/trains/*
   152→│   │   ├── depots.ts         # GET /api/depots/*
   153→│   │   ├── services.ts       # GET /api/services/*
   154→│   │   ├── allocations.ts    # GET/POST /api/allocations/*
   155→│   │   └── simulation.ts     # POST /api/simulation/*
   156→│   │
   157→│   ├── services/
   158→│   │   ├── trainService.ts
   159→│   │   ├── depotService.ts
   160→│   │   ├── allocationService.ts
   161→│   │   ├── simulationService.ts
   162→│   │   └── solverClient.ts   # HTTP client to Python solver
   163→│   │
   164→│   ├── db/
   165→│   │   ├── client.ts         # PostgreSQL client (pg or drizzle)
   166→│   │   ├── schema.ts         # Table definitions
   167→│   │   └── seed.ts           # Seed data loader
   168→│   │
   169→│   └── types/
   170→│       └── index.ts          # Shared types
   171→│
   172→└── package.json
   173→```
   174→
   175→### 2.3 Solver Structure
   176→
   177→```
   178→solver/
   179→├── src/
   180→│   ├── main.py               # FastAPI server
   181→│   ├── models/
   182→│   │   ├── train.py          # Train dataclass
   183→│   │   ├── service.py        # Service dataclass
   184→│   │   └── recommendation.py # Output dataclass
   185→│   │
   186→│   ├── engine/
   187→│   │   ├── allocator.py      # CP-SAT allocation model
   188→│   │   ├── scorer.py         # Train scoring logic
   189→│   │   └── justifier.py      # Justification generator
   190→│   │
   191→│   └── api/
   192→│       └── routes.py         # POST /recommend, POST /simulate
   193→│
   194→├── requirements.txt
   195→└── Dockerfile
   196→```
   197→
   198→---
   199→
   200→## 3. Data Architecture
   201→
   202→### 3.1 Database Schema
   203→
   204→```sql
   205→-- Core entities
   206→CREATE TABLE fleets (
   207→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   208→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   209→    manufacturer VARCHAR(50),
   210→    pm1_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   211→    pm2_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   212→    hm_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   213→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   214→);
   215→
   216→CREATE TABLE depots (
   217→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   218→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   219→    code VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
   220→    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   221→    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   222→    capacity_tracks INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
   223→    capacity_manhours INTEGER DEFAULT 80,
   224→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   225→);
   226→
   227→CREATE TABLE trains (
   228→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   229→    train_id VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- Business ID: UTE-3400-012
   230→    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
   231→    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   232→    current_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   233→    target_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   234→    fitness_status VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'fit' CHECK (fitness_status IN ('fit', 'unfit')),
   235→    last_maintenance_date DATE,
   236→    next_maintenance_km INTEGER,
   237→    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   238→    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   239→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   240→    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   241→);
   242→
   243→CREATE TABLE services (
   244→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   245→    service_code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- IC 501
   246→    origin VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   247→    destination VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   248→    distance_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   249→    departure_time TIME NOT NULL,
   250→    arrival_time TIME NOT NULL,
   251→    service_type VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'regional',  -- regional, intercity
   252→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   253→);
   254→
   255→CREATE TABLE allocations (
   256→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   257→    service_id UUID REFERENCES services(id),
   258→    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   259→    allocation_date DATE NOT NULL,
   260→    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
   261→    score INTEGER,
   262→    justifications JSONB,  -- Array of reason strings
   263→    created_by VARCHAR(100),
   264→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   265→    UNIQUE(service_id, allocation_date)
   266→);
   267→
   268→-- Mileage history for trends
   269→CREATE TABLE mileage_history (
   270→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   271→    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   272→    recorded_date DATE NOT NULL,
   273→    km_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
   274→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   275→);
   276→
   277→-- Maintenance schedules
   278→CREATE TABLE maintenance_schedules (
   279→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   280→    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   281→    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   282→    maintenance_type VARCHAR(10) CHECK (maintenance_type IN ('PM1', 'PM2', 'HM', 'OVH')),
   283→    scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
   284→    duration_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   285→    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'scheduled' CHECK (status IN ('scheduled', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
   286→    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   287→);
   288→
   289→-- Indexes for performance
   290→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
   291→CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(base_depot_id);
   292→CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_date ON allocations(allocation_date);
   293→CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_status ON allocations(status);
   294→CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_history(train_id, recorded_date);
   295→```
   296→
   297→### 3.2 Data Flow
   298→
   299→```
   300→┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
   301→│   Frontend  │────▶│   API       │────▶│  Database   │
   302→│   (React)   │◀────│  (Fastify)  │◀────│ (PostgreSQL)│
   303→└─────────────┘     └──────┬──────┘     └─────────────┘
   304→                           │
   305→                           ▼
   306→                   ┌─────────────┐
   307→                   │   Solver    │
   308→                   │  (Python)   │
   309→                   └─────────────┘
   310→```
   311→
   312→**Read Path (Dashboard):**
   313→1. Frontend requests `/api/dashboard/summary`
   314→2. API queries aggregated KPIs from DB
   315→3. Response cached in Redis (60s TTL)
   316→4. Frontend renders immediately
   317→
   318→**Write Path (Allocation):**
   319→1. User selects service, clicks "Recommend"
   320→2. Frontend calls `POST /api/allocations/recommend`
   321→3. API calls Solver service with service details
   322→4. Solver returns ranked recommendations with justifications
   323→5. User confirms, API writes to allocations table
   324→6. Frontend updates optimistically
   325→
   326→---
   327→
   328→## 4. API Specification
   329→
   330→### 4.1 REST Endpoints
   331→
   332→#### Dashboard
   333→```
   334→GET /api/dashboard/summary
   335→Response: {
   336→  totalTrains: number,
   337→  availableTrains: number,
   338→  inMaintenanceCount: number,
   339→  kmVariance: number,
   340→  varianceStatus: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red'
   341→}
   342→
   343→GET /api/dashboard/depot-capacity
   344→Response: {
   345→  depots: [{
   346→    id: string,
   347→    name: string,
   348→    code: string,
   349→    currentOccupancy: number,
   350→    maxCapacity: number,
   351→    percentUsed: number
   352→  }]
   353→}
   354→```
   355→
   356→#### Trains
   357→```
   358→GET /api/trains
   359→Query: ?fleet=uuid&depot=uuid&status=fit
   360→Response: { trains: Train[] }
   361→
   362→GET /api/trains/:id
   363→Response: Train
   364→
   365→GET /api/trains/:id/history
   366→Response: { history: MileagePoint[] }
   367→```
   368→
   369→#### Allocation
   370→```
   371→GET /api/services/pending
   372→Query: ?date=2025-01-15
   373→Response: { services: Service[] }
   374→
   375→POST /api/allocations/recommend
   376→Body: { serviceId: string, date: string }
   377→Response: {
   378→  recommendations: [{
   379→    trainId: string,
   380→    score: number,
   381→    justifications: string[]
   382→  }],
   383→  solverTimeMs: number
   384→}
   385→
   386→POST /api/allocations
   387→Body: { serviceId: string, trainId: string, date: string }
   388→Response: { allocation: Allocation }
   389→```
   390→
   391→#### Analysis
   392→```
   393→GET /api/balancing/distribution
   394→Query: ?fleet=uuid
   395→Response: {
   396→  average: number,
   397→  variance: number,
   398→  distribution: { kmRange: string, count: number }[],
   399→  outliers: { trainId: string, delta: number }[]
   400→}
   401→
   402→POST /api/simulation/create
   403→Body: { name: string, baseDate: string }
   404→Response: { scenarioId: string }
   405→
   406→POST /api/simulation/:id/run
   407→Body: { changes: AllocationChange[] }
   408→Response: {
   409→  projectedVariance: number,
   410→  projectedDeadRunning: number,
   411→  comparison: MetricComparison[]
   412→}
   413→```
   414→
   415→### 4.2 Solver API (Internal)
   416→
   417→```
   418→POST /recommend
   419→Body: {
   420→  service: { id, distance_km, origin, destination },
   421→  candidates: Train[],
   422→  constraints: {
   423→    prioritize_balancing: boolean,
   424→    max_dead_running_km: number
   425→  }
   426→}
   427→Response: {
   428→  recommendations: [{
   429→    train_id: string,
   430→    score: number,
   431→    justifications: string[],
   432→    dead_running_km: number
   433→  }],
   434→  solver_time_ms: number
   435→}
   436→```
   437→
   438→---
   439→
   440→## 5. Technology Decisions
   441→
   442→### 5.1 Frontend Stack
   443→
   444→| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
   445→|------------|---------|-------------------|
   446→| Next.js | 14.x | App Router, RSC, SSR |
   447→| React | 18.x | Concurrent features, suspense |
   448→| TypeScript | 5.x | Type safety, IDE support |
   449→| Tailwind CSS | 3.x | Utility-first, rapid prototyping |
   450→| shadcn/ui | latest | Pre-built accessible components |
   451→| @zazcart/toolkit | local | State management without React hook issues |
   452→| Recharts | 2.x | Simple charting, React integration |
   453→| Mapbox GL / Leaflet | latest | Interactive maps |
   454→
   455→### 5.2 Backend Stack
   456→
   457→| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
   458→|------------|---------|-------------------|
   459→| Node.js | 20.x LTS | Stable, TypeScript support |
   460→| Fastify | 4.x | Performance, schema validation |
   461→| TypeScript | 5.x | Shared types with frontend |
   462→| PostgreSQL | 16.x | JSONB, reliability |
   463→| Redis | 7.x | Caching, session storage |
   464→| Drizzle ORM | latest | Type-safe SQL, migrations |
   465→
   466→### 5.3 Solver Stack
   467→
   468→| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
   469→|------------|---------|-------------------|
   470→| Python | 3.11 | OR-Tools compatibility |
   471→| FastAPI | 0.100+ | Async, auto-docs |
   472→| OR-Tools | 9.x | Google's optimization suite |
   473→| Pydantic | 2.x | Data validation |
   474→| uvicorn | latest | ASGI server |
   475→
   476→---
   477→
   478→## 6. Integration Architecture
   479→
   480→### 6.1 Frontend ↔ Backend
   481→
   482→```
   483→[Next.js App]
   484→     │
   485→     │  fetch('/api/...')
   486→     │  Headers: Content-Type: application/json
   487→     ▼
   488→[Fastify Server]
   489→     │
   490→     │  Route handlers call services
   491→     ▼
   492→[Service Layer]
   493→     │
   494→     │  Drizzle ORM queries
   495→     ▼
   496→[PostgreSQL]
   497→```
   498→
   499→**Error Handling:**
   500→- API returns standardized error format: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`
   501→- Frontend uses try/catch with toast notifications
   502→- Network errors show retry option
   503→
   504→### 6.2 Backend ↔ Solver
   505→
   506→```
   507→[Fastify Service]
   508→     │
   509→     │  HTTP POST (axios/fetch)
   510→     │  Timeout: 30 seconds
   511→     ▼
   512→[Python FastAPI]
   513→     │
   514→     │  CP-SAT model execution
   515→     ▼
   516→[Response with justifications]
   517→```
   518→
   519→**Resilience:**
   520→- Solver timeout: 30 seconds hard limit
   521→- Fallback: Return top 3 trains by simple scoring if solver fails
   522→- Cache: Store recent recommendations in Redis (5 min TTL)
   523→
   524→---
   525→
   526→## 7. Non-Functional Architecture
   527→
   528→### 7.1 Performance
   529→
   530→| Metric | Target | Implementation |
   531→|--------|--------|----------------|
   532→| Dashboard load | < 2s | SSR + cache |
   533→| API response | < 500ms | Index optimization |
   534→| Map render | < 2s | Tile caching, clustering |
   535→| Solver response | < 30s | CP-SAT with timeout |
   536→
   537→### 7.2 Caching Strategy
   538→
   539→```
   540→Redis Cache:
   541→├── dashboard:summary (TTL: 60s)
   542→├── trains:list (TTL: 30s)
   543→├── depots:list (TTL: 300s)
   544→├── solver:recommend:{hash} (TTL: 300s)
   545→└── session:{id} (TTL: 3600s)
   546→```
   547→
   548→### 7.3 Demo Stability
   549→
   550→| Scenario | Mitigation |
   551→|----------|------------|
   552→| Database down | Pre-loaded fallback JSON |
   553→| Solver timeout | Simple ranking fallback |
   554→| Network issue | Service worker cache |
   555→| UI crash | Error boundary with retry |
   556→
   557→---
   558→
   559→## 8. Development Setup
   560→
   561→### 8.1 Monorepo Structure
   562→
   563→```
   564→mms/
   565→├── package.json              # Root package (workspaces)
   566→├── apps/
   567→│   ├── web/                  # Next.js frontend
   568→│   └── api/                  # Fastify backend
   569→├── packages/
   570→│   ├── types/                # Shared TypeScript types
   571→│   └── utils/                # Shared utilities
   572→├── solver/                   # Python solver (separate)
   573→├── docker-compose.yml        # Local dev environment
   574→└── scripts/
   575→    ├── seed.ts               # Database seeding
   576→    └── dev.sh                # Start all services
   577→```
   578→
   579→### 8.2 Local Development
   580→
   581→```bash
   582→# Start infrastructure
   583→docker-compose up -d postgres redis
   584→
   585→# Start API
   586→cd apps/api && bun run dev
   587→
   588→# Start Solver
   589→cd solver && uvicorn src.main:app --reload
   590→
   591→# Start Frontend
   592→cd apps/web && bun run dev
   593→```
   594→
   595→### 8.3 Environment Variables
   596→
   597→```env
   598→# Database
   599→DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms
   600→
   601→# Redis
   602→REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
   603→
   604→# Solver
   605→SOLVER_URL=http://localhost:8000
   606→
   607→# Mapbox (optional, can use Leaflet)
   608→NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN=pk.xxx
   609→```
   610→
   611→---
   612→
   613→## 9. Security Considerations (Demo Scope)
   614→
   615→### 9.1 Demo Simplifications
   616→
   617→| Production Need | Demo Approach |
   618→|-----------------|---------------|
   619→| Authentication | None (demo mode) |
   620→| Authorization | None (single user) |
   621→| HTTPS | HTTP for local dev |
   622→| Input validation | Basic schema validation |
   623→| Rate limiting | None |
   624→
   625→### 9.2 Data Protection
   626→
   627→- No real customer data (seed data only)
   628→- No PII in seed data
   629→- Demo reset between presentations
   630→
   631→---
   632→
   633→## 10. Deployment Architecture (Demo)
   634→
   635→### 10.1 Demo Environment
   636→
   637→```
   638→┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
   639→│           Demo Laptop                    │
   640→│  ┌───────────────────────────────────┐  │
   641→│  │  Docker Compose                    │  │
   642→│  │  ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ ┌─────┐ │  │
   643→│  │  │Next │ │API  │ │Solver│ │PG   │ │  │
   644→│  │  │:3000│ │:4000│ │:8000 │ │:5432│ │  │
   645→│  │  └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘ └─────┘ │  │
   646→│  └───────────────────────────────────┘  │
   647→│                                          │
   648→│  Browser → http://localhost:3000        │
   649→└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
   650→```
   651→
   652→### 10.2 Demo Reset Script
   653→
   654→```bash
   655→#!/bin/bash
   656→# Reset demo to clean state
   657→docker-compose down -v
   658→docker-compose up -d
   659→sleep 5
   660→bun run seed
   661→echo "Demo ready!"
   662→```
   663→
   664→---
   665→
   666→## 11. AI Agent Guidelines
   667→
   668→### 11.1 Code Patterns
   669→
   670→**DO:**
   671→- Use TypeScript strict mode
   672→- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
   673→- Use @zazcart/toolkit for frontend state
   674→- Use shadcn/ui components
   675→- Follow existing file naming conventions
   676→- Add JSDoc comments for public functions
   677→
   678→**DON'T:**
   679→- Use useState for application state (use toolkit)
   680→- Create new component libraries
   681→- Add authentication logic
   682→- Implement features outside PRD scope
   683→- Add time estimates to code comments
   684→
   685→### 11.2 File Naming
   686→
   687→```
   688→Components: PascalCase.tsx (e.g., TrainCard.tsx)
   689→Utilities: camelCase.ts (e.g., formatKm.ts)
   690→Routes: lowercase/route.ts (e.g., trains/route.ts)
   691→Types: PascalCase.ts (e.g., Train.ts)
   692→```
   693→
   694→### 11.3 Testing Priority
   695→
   696→1. Solver logic (unit tests)
   697→2. API endpoints (integration tests)
   698→3. Critical UI flows (Playwright)
   699→
   700→---
   701→

   703→*Date: 2025-12-28*
   704→

# Architecture Decision Document - MMS Metro Mileage Management System

_This document defines the technical architecture for the MMS railway fleet management demo system._

---

## 1. System Overview

### 1.1 Architecture Vision

MMS is a **demo-focused system** designed to win a competitive bid through a 15-minute presentation. The architecture prioritizes:

1. **Demo Stability** - 15 minutes without crashes
2. **Visual Impact** - Fast rendering, smooth interactions
3. **Domain Credibility** - Correct railway terminology, realistic behavior
4. **Technical Proof** - Working solver, real-time recommendations

### 1.2 High-Level Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         BROWSER                                  │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                    Next.js 14 App                        │    │
│  │  ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐    │    │
│  │  │Dashboard │ │   Map    │ │Allocation│ │ Analysis │    │    │
│  │  │  Page    │ │Component │ │  Page    │ │   Page   │    │    │
│  │  └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘    │    │
│  │                      ↓ HTTP/REST                         │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         SERVER                                   │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                  Fastify API Server                      │    │
│  │  ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐    │    │
│  │  │Dashboard │ │  Trains  │ │Allocation│ │Simulation│    │    │
│  │  │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │ │  Routes  │    │    │
│  │  └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘    │    │
│  │                      ↓                                   │    │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │    │
│  │  │              Service Layer                       │    │    │
│  │  │  TrainService | AllocationService | SolverClient│    │    │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                    ↓                           ↓                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐       ┌─────────────────────────┐      │
│  │    PostgreSQL 16    │       │   Python Solver Service │      │
│  │  ┌───────────────┐  │       │  ┌───────────────────┐  │      │
│  │  │    trains     │  │       │  │  OR-Tools CP-SAT  │  │      │
│  │  │    depots     │  │       │  │  Recommendation   │  │      │
│  │  │   services    │  │       │  │     Engine        │  │      │
│  │  │  allocations  │  │       │  └───────────────────┘  │      │
│  │  └───────────────┘  │       │         ↑ HTTP          │      │
│  └─────────────────────┘       └─────────────────────────┘      │
│            ↑                                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────┐                                        │
│  │       Redis         │ (session cache, solver results)        │
│  └─────────────────────┘                                        │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Frontend Framework | Next.js 14 (App Router) | SSR for initial load, React ecosystem |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid development, consistent design |
| Backend Framework | Fastify | Performance, TypeScript support |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | Reliable, JSON support, demo-proven |
| Solver | Python + OR-Tools | Best-in-class optimization, justifications |
| State Management | @zazcart/toolkit | Avoids React hook pitfalls |
| API Style | REST | Simplicity for demo scope |

---

## 2. Component Architecture

### 2.1 Frontend Structure

```
src/
├── app/                      # Next.js App Router
│   ├── layout.tsx            # Root layout with nav
│   ├── page.tsx              # Dashboard (home)
│   ├── fleet/
│   │   ├── page.tsx          # Train list
│   │   └── [id]/page.tsx     # Train details
│   ├── allocation/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Service allocation
│   ├── analysis/
│   │   └── page.tsx          # Km balancing, simulation
│   └── schedule/
│       └── page.tsx          # Maintenance Gantt
│
├── components/
│   ├── ui/                   # shadcn/ui components
│   ├── dashboard/
│   │   ├── KPICard.tsx
│   │   ├── KPIGrid.tsx
│   │   └── DepotCapacity.tsx
│   ├── map/
│   │   ├── FleetMap.tsx
│   │   ├── TrainMarker.tsx
│   │   └── DepotMarker.tsx
│   ├── allocation/
│   │   ├── ServiceList.tsx
│   │   ├── RecommendationCard.tsx
│   │   └── JustificationPanel.tsx
│   ├── analysis/
│   │   ├── DistributionChart.tsx
│   │   ├── ScenarioComparison.tsx
│   │   └── TrainRanking.tsx
│   └── gantt/
│       ├── MaintenanceGantt.tsx
│       └── GanttBlock.tsx
│
├── lib/
│   ├── api.ts                # API client
│   ├── types.ts              # TypeScript interfaces
│   └── utils.ts              # Utilities
│
└── stores/                   # @zazcart/toolkit state
    ├── fleetStore.ts
    ├── allocationStore.ts
    └── simulationStore.ts
```

### 2.2 Backend Structure

```
server/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Fastify server entry
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── dashboard.ts      # GET /api/dashboard/*
│   │   ├── trains.ts         # GET/POST /api/trains/*
│   │   ├── depots.ts         # GET /api/depots/*
│   │   ├── services.ts       # GET /api/services/*
│   │   ├── allocations.ts    # GET/POST /api/allocations/*
│   │   └── simulation.ts     # POST /api/simulation/*
│   │
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── trainService.ts
│   │   ├── depotService.ts
│   │   ├── allocationService.ts
│   │   ├── simulationService.ts
│   │   └── solverClient.ts   # HTTP client to Python solver
│   │
│   ├── db/
│   │   ├── client.ts         # PostgreSQL client (pg or drizzle)
│   │   ├── schema.ts         # Table definitions
│   │   └── seed.ts           # Seed data loader
│   │
│   └── types/
│       └── index.ts          # Shared types
│
└── package.json
```

### 2.3 Solver Structure

```
solver/
├── src/
│   ├── main.py               # FastAPI server
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── train.py          # Train dataclass
│   │   ├── service.py        # Service dataclass
│   │   └── recommendation.py # Output dataclass
│   │
│   ├── engine/
│   │   ├── allocator.py      # CP-SAT allocation model
│   │   ├── scorer.py         # Train scoring logic
│   │   └── justifier.py      # Justification generator
│   │
│   └── api/
│       └── routes.py         # POST /recommend, POST /simulate
│
├── requirements.txt
└── Dockerfile
```

---

## 3. Data Architecture

### 3.1 Database Schema

```sql
-- Core entities
CREATE TABLE fleets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(50),
    pm1_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    pm2_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    hm_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE depots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    code VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    capacity_tracks INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
    capacity_manhours INTEGER DEFAULT 80,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE trains (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- Business ID: UTE-3400-012
    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    current_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    target_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    fitness_status VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'fit' CHECK (fitness_status IN ('fit', 'unfit')),
    last_maintenance_date DATE,
    next_maintenance_km INTEGER,
    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE services (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    service_code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- IC 501
    origin VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    destination VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    distance_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    departure_time TIME NOT NULL,
    arrival_time TIME NOT NULL,
    service_type VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'regional',  -- regional, intercity
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE allocations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    service_id UUID REFERENCES services(id),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    allocation_date DATE NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
    score INTEGER,
    justifications JSONB,  -- Array of reason strings
    created_by VARCHAR(100),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(service_id, allocation_date)
);

-- Mileage history for trends
CREATE TABLE mileage_history (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    recorded_date DATE NOT NULL,
    km_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Maintenance schedules
CREATE TABLE maintenance_schedules (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    maintenance_type VARCHAR(10) CHECK (maintenance_type IN ('PM1', 'PM2', 'HM', 'OVH')),
    scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
    duration_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'scheduled' CHECK (status IN ('scheduled', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Indexes for performance
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(base_depot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_date ON allocations(allocation_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_status ON allocations(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_history(train_id, recorded_date);
```

### 3.2 Data Flow

```
┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   Frontend  │────▶│   API       │────▶│  Database   │
│   (React)   │◀────│  (Fastify)  │◀────│ (PostgreSQL)│
└─────────────┘     └──────┬──────┘     └─────────────┘
                           │
                           ▼
                   ┌─────────────┐
                   │   Solver    │
                   │  (Python)   │
                   └─────────────┘
```

**Read Path (Dashboard):**
1. Frontend requests `/api/dashboard/summary`
2. API queries aggregated KPIs from DB
3. Response cached in Redis (60s TTL)
4. Frontend renders immediately

**Write Path (Allocation):**
1. User selects service, clicks "Recommend"
2. Frontend calls `POST /api/allocations/recommend`
3. API calls Solver service with service details
4. Solver returns ranked recommendations with justifications
5. User confirms, API writes to allocations table
6. Frontend updates optimistically

## 4. API Specification

### 4.1 REST Endpoints

#### Dashboard
```
GET /api/dashboard/summary
Response: {
  totalTrains: number,
  availableTrains: number,
  inMaintenanceCount: number,
  kmVariance: number,
  varianceStatus: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red'
}

GET /api/dashboard/depot-capacity
Response: {
  depots: [{
    id: string,
    name: string,
    code: string,
    currentOccupancy: number,
    maxCapacity: number,
    percentUsed: number
  }]
}
```

#### Trains
```
GET /api/trains
Query: ?fleet=uuid&depot=uuid&status=fit
Response: { trains: Train[] }

GET /api/trains/:id
Response: Train

GET /api/trains/:id/history
Response: { history: MileagePoint[] }
```

#### Allocation
```
GET /api/services/pending
Query: ?date=2025-01-15
Response: { services: Service[] }

POST /api/allocations/recommend
Body: { serviceId: string, date: string }
Response: {
  recommendations: [{
    trainId: string,
    score: number,
    justifications: string[]
  }],
  solverTimeMs: number
}

POST /api/allocations
Body: { serviceId: string, trainId: string, date: string }
Response: { allocation: Allocation }
```

#### Analysis
```
GET /api/balancing/distribution
Query: ?fleet=uuid
Response: {
  average: number,
  variance: number,
  distribution: { kmRange: string, count: number }[],
  outliers: { trainId: string, delta: number }[]
}

POST /api/simulation/create
Body: { name: string, baseDate: string }
Response: { scenarioId: string }

POST /api/simulation/:id/run
Body: { changes: AllocationChange[] }
Response: {
  projectedVariance: number,
  projectedDeadRunning: number,
  comparison: MetricComparison[]
}
```

### 4.2 Solver API (Internal)

```
POST /recommend
Body: {
  service: { id, distance_km, origin, destination },
  candidates: Train[],
  constraints: {
    prioritize_balancing: boolean,
    max_dead_running_km: number
  }
}
Response: {
  recommendations: [{
    train_id: string,
    score: number,
    justifications: string[],
    dead_running_km: number
  }],
  solver_time_ms: number
}
```

## 6. Integration Architecture

### 6.1 Frontend ↔ Backend

```
[Next.js App]
     │
     │  fetch('/api/...')
     │  Headers: Content-Type: application/json
     ▼
[Fastify Server]
     │
     │  Route handlers call services
     ▼
[Service Layer]
     │
     │  Drizzle ORM queries
     ▼
[PostgreSQL]
```

**Error Handling:**
- API returns standardized error format: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`
- Frontend uses try/catch with toast notifications
- Network errors show retry option

### 6.2 Backend ↔ Solver

```
[Fastify Service]
     │
     │  HTTP POST (axios/fetch)
     │  Timeout: 30 seconds
     ▼
[Python FastAPI]
     │
     │  CP-SAT model execution
     ▼
[Response with justifications]
```

**Resilience:**
- Solver timeout: 30 seconds hard limit
- Fallback: Return top 3 trains by simple scoring if solver fails
- Cache: Store recent recommendations in Redis (5 min TTL)

## 7. Non-Functional Architecture

### 7.1 Performance

| Metric | Target | Implementation |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| Dashboard load | < 2s | SSR + cache |
| API response | < 500ms | Index optimization |
| Map render | < 2s | Tile caching, clustering |
| Solver response | < 30s | CP-SAT with timeout |

### 7.2 Caching Strategy

```
Redis Cache:
├── dashboard:summary (TTL: 60s)
├── trains:list (TTL: 30s)
├── depots:list (TTL: 300s)
├── solver:recommend:{hash} (TTL: 300s)
└── session:{id} (TTL: 3600s)
```

### 7.3 Demo Stability

| Scenario | Mitigation |
|----------|------------|
| Database down | Pre-loaded fallback JSON |
| Solver timeout | Simple ranking fallback |
| Network issue | Service worker cache |
| UI crash | Error boundary with retry |

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis

# Start API
cd apps/api && bun run dev

# Start Solver
cd solver && uvicorn src.main:app --reload

# Start Frontend
cd apps/web && bun run dev
```

## 11. AI Agent Guidelines

### 11.1 Code Patterns

**DO:**
- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
- Use @zazcart/toolkit for frontend state
- Use shadcn/ui components
- Follow existing file naming conventions
- Add JSDoc comments for public functions

**DON'T:**
- Use useState for application state (use toolkit)
- Create new component libraries
- Add authentication logic
- Implement features outside PRD scope
- Add time estimates to code comments

### 11.2 File Naming

```
Components: PascalCase.tsx (e.g., TrainCard.tsx)
Utilities: camelCase.ts (e.g., formatKm.ts)
Routes: lowercase/route.ts (e.g., trains/route.ts)
Types: PascalCase.ts (e.g., Train.ts)
```

### 11.3 Testing Priority

1. Solver logic (unit tests)
2. API endpoints (integration tests)
3. Critical UI flows (Playwright)

    23→## Executive Summary
    24→
    25→**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units), bridging Operations and Maintenance via predictive, data-driven planning.
    26→
    27→### Vision
    28→
    29→Create a functional demo in **3 months** that:
    30→- Demonstrates technical competence in railway fleet management
    31→- Proves deep understanding of the railway domain

    34→### Success Metric

    37→
    38→### Core Problem
    39→
    40→Railway operators face three critical pain points:
    41→1. **Over-running trains** degrade faster, increasing maintenance cost
    42→2. **Under-running trains** represent idle capital
    43→3. **Manual planning in Excel** doesn't scale for 100+ train fleets
    44→
    45→MMS addresses all three points through intelligent mileage balancing, maintenance scheduling, and operations coordination.
    46→
    47→### What Makes This Special
    48→
    49→| Differentiator | Why It Matters |

    52→| **Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface** | Eliminates silos, reduces rework |
    53→| **Visible dead running cost** | Hidden cost made explicit and optimized |
    54→| **Accessible what-if simulation** | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
    55→| **Visible decision justification** | Trust, not black box - every recommendation explains why |

   233→### Journey 1: Diana - Allocating a Train to Service
   234→
   235→**The Story:**
   236→Diana arrives at the operations center for her morning shift. The timetable shows that service IC 501 (Lisbon to Porto, 336 km) needs a train assigned for tomorrow's departure. She opens MMS and navigates to the Allocation screen.
   237→
   238→**Scene 1: The Request**
   239→Diana selects service IC 501 from the service list. The system shows the service details: departure at 08:15, expected arrival 11:45, route profile indicating high-speed segments.
   240→
   241→**Scene 2: The Recommendation**
   242→With one click on "Recommend," MMS presents her with a ranked list of 5 suitable trains. The top recommendation, UTE-3400-012, shows a score of 94/100 with clear justifications:
   243→- "Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)"
   244→- "Service ends 5km from Porto maintenance depot"
   245→- "PM1 scheduled in 3 days, Porto depot has available slot"
   246→- "Aptitude confirmed for North line"
   247→
   248→**Scene 3: The Decision**
   249→Diana reviews the justifications and appreciates that the system explains WHY this train is recommended. She confirms the allocation with a single click.
   250→
   251→**Scene 4: The Resolution**
   252→The system updates the allocation status, and Diana moves on to the next service. Total time: under 2 minutes, fewer than 5 clicks.

   263→### Journey 2: Paul - Identifying and Resolving Mileage Imbalance
   264→
   265→**The Story:**
   266→Paul starts his Monday morning by checking the fleet health. He's been worried about the mileage variance creeping up over the past few weeks.
   267→
   268→**Scene 1: The Discovery**
   269→Opening the Dashboard, Paul immediately sees the km variance indicator showing 18% in yellow - above the ideal 10% threshold. He clicks on the indicator for details.
   270→
   271→**Scene 2: The Analysis**
   272→The Balancing screen shows a histogram of fleet km distribution. Paul can clearly see 5 trains well above average (over-running) and 4 trains significantly below (under-running). He hovers over train UTE-2200-015 and sees it's 25% below the fleet average.
   273→
   274→**Scene 3: The Simulation**
   275→Paul clicks "New Scenario" to test his hypothesis. He adjusts future allocations to prioritize the under-running trains for high-km services. The simulation runs in under 10 seconds and shows the projected impact: variance dropping from 18% to 14% over the next 30 days.
   276→
   277→**Scene 4: The Comparison**
   278→Using side-by-side comparison, Paul sees that his adjustment reduces variance but increases dead running by 3%. He decides the trade-off is worth it.
   279→
   280→**Scene 5: The Resolution**
   281→Paul applies the scenario changes and documents his decision rationale. Total time: under 10 minutes.

   283→**Requirements Revealed:**
   284→- Dashboard with variance indicators
   285→- Km distribution visualization (histogram/box plot)
   286→- Train ranking by km delta
   287→- Scenario creation and simulation
   288→- Side-by-side comparison view
   289→- Apply scenario functionality
   290→
   291→---
   292→
   293→### Journey 3: Paul - Planning Maintenance Schedule
   294→
   295→**The Story:**
   296→Paul needs to schedule preventive maintenances for the coming month. He wants to ensure depot capacity isn't exceeded.
   297→
   298→**Scene 1: The Overview**
   299→Paul opens the Maintenance Gantt view showing the next 4 weeks. Each train has a timeline bar, and scheduled maintenances appear as colored blocks (PM1 in light blue, PM2 in dark blue, HM in red).
   300→
   301→**Scene 2: The Conflict**
   302→In week 2, Paul notices the Lisbon depot showing red - capacity at 100%. Three trains are scheduled for PM1 on the same day.
   303→
   304→**Scene 3: The Resolution**
   305→Paul drags one of the PM1 blocks from week 2 to week 3. The system immediately validates: green checkmark - no capacity conflict, train still within maintenance window tolerance.
   306→
   307→**Scene 4: The Confirmation**
   308→Paul saves the change. The system confirms the update and the capacity indicator turns green. Total time: under 5 minutes.
   309→
   310→**Requirements Revealed:**
   311→- Gantt timeline visualization
   312→- Drag-and-drop scheduling
   313→- Real-time conflict validation
   314→- Depot capacity indicators
   315→- Maintenance window tolerance checking
   316→
   317→---
   318→
   319→### Journey 4: Gabriel - Executive Review
   320→
   321→**The Story:**
   322→Gabriel has 30 minutes before a board meeting. He needs to understand fleet performance for the past month and identify any concerns.
   323→
   324→**Scene 1: The Dashboard**
   325→Gabriel opens MMS on his tablet. The Dashboard immediately shows four KPI cards:
   326→- Total trains: 112
   327→- Available: 98 (87%)
   328→- In Maintenance: 14
   329→- Km Variance: 18% ⚠️
   330→
   331→**Scene 2: The Drill-Down**
   332→The yellow warning on variance catches his attention. He taps it to see the trend chart showing variance increasing over the past 6 months.
   333→
   334→**Scene 3: The Context**
   335→Scrolling down, Gabriel sees the depot cards. Two depots (Lisbon and Porto) show near-full capacity. He taps Lisbon depot to see which trains are stationed there.
   336→
   337→**Scene 4: The Insight**
   338→Gabriel now has the talking points he needs: "We're seeing increased variance that maintenance is addressing, and Lisbon depot is approaching capacity - we may need to redistribute fleet." Total time: under 15 minutes.
   339→
   340→**Requirements Revealed:**
   341→- KPI cards with status indicators
   342→- Trend visualization over time
   343→- Drill-down from summary to details
   344→- Depot capacity visualization
   345→- Mobile-responsive design
   346→
   347→---
   348→
   349→### Journey Requirements Summary
   350→
   351→| Journey | Primary Persona | Key Capabilities Revealed |
   352→|---------|-----------------|---------------------------|
   353→| Allocate Train | Diana (Dispatcher) | Recommendation engine, justifications, quick confirmation |
   354→| Resolve Imbalance | Paul (Planner) | Distribution charts, simulation, scenario comparison |
   355→| Plan Maintenance | Paul (Planner) | Gantt view, drag-drop, conflict validation |
   356→| Executive Review | Gabriel (Manager) | KPIs, trends, drill-down, mobile responsive |
   357→
   358→**Critical Capabilities Identified:**
   359→1. **Recommendation Engine** - Ranked train suggestions with justifications
   360→2. **Km Balancing Visualization** - Distribution charts, variance indicators
   361→3. **Scenario Simulation** - What-if testing with side-by-side comparison
   362→4. **Maintenance Gantt** - Timeline view with drag-drop scheduling
   363→5. **Dashboard KPIs** - Traffic-light status, trend visualization
   364→6. **Drill-Down Navigation** - Fleet → Train → Component → History
   365→7. **Real-Time Validation** - Instant feedback on changes
   366→
   367→---
   368→
   369→## Domain Requirements
   370→
   371→### Railway Domain Terminology
   372→
   373→All UI labels and messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology:
   374→
   375→| UI Label | Domain Term | NOT Acceptable |
   376→|----------|-------------|----------------|
   377→| Rolling Stock | Rolling Stock | Vehicle, Train, Asset |
   378→| Fleet | Fleet | Group, Class |
   379→| Depot | Depot | Garage, Workshop |
   380→| Preventive Maintenance | PM1, PM2, HM | Light/Heavy Service |
   381→| Train Fitness | Fitness | Availability |
   382→| Run Assignment | Allocation | Dispatch |
   383→| Dead Running | Dead Running | Empty Movement |
   384→| Service | Service | Route, Trip |
   385→
   386→### Maintenance Hierarchy
   387→
   388→```
   389→Fleet Type
   390→ └─ Maintenance Profile
   391→     ├─ PM1 (every 15,000 km) - Light preventive
   392→     ├─ PM2 (every 45,000 km) - Medium preventive
   393→     ├─ HM (every 90,000 km) - Heavy overhaul
   394→     └─ Overhaul (every 6 years) - Full refurbishment
   395→```
   396→
   397→### Constraint Priority (Solver Must Respect)
   398→
   399→1. **Train Fitness** - Unfit trains never assigned (hard constraint)
   400→2. **Maintenance Windows** - Scheduled maintenance blocks availability
   401→3. **Resource Limits** - Man-hours, track slots per depot
   402→4. **Km Balancing** - Optimize within above constraints
   403→
   404→### Entity Relationships
   405→
   406→```
   407→Fleet (1) ---- (*) Train
   408→  |                 |
   409→  |                 +---- (*) RunAssignment
   410→  |                 |
   411→  |                 +---- (1) TrainFitness [daily state]
   412→  |
   413→  +---- (1) BaseDepot
   414→  |
   415→  +---- (1) MaintenanceProfile
   416→```
   417→
   418→---
   419→
   420→## Functional Requirements
   421→
   422→### FR-1: Dashboard Module
   423→
   424→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   425→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   426→| FR-1.1 | Display fleet summary KPIs | P0 | 4 KPI cards: total, available, in maintenance, variance |
   427→| FR-1.2 | Show variance indicator with traffic-light | P0 | Green ≤10%, Yellow 10-20%, Red >20% |
   428→| FR-1.3 | Display depot capacity cards | P1 | Each depot shows current/max capacity |
   429→| FR-1.4 | Show maintenance alerts | P1 | Upcoming PM/HM in next 7 days |
   430→| FR-1.5 | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 | Click any element to see details |
   431→
   432→### FR-2: Map Module
   433→
   434→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   435→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   436→| FR-2.1 | Display train positions on map | P0 | All active trains visible as markers |
   437→| FR-2.2 | Color-code by status | P0 | Available=green, In-service=blue, Maintenance=red |
   438→| FR-2.3 | Show depot locations | P1 | Depot icons with capacity indicator |
   439→| FR-2.4 | Enable train selection | P0 | Click marker shows train card |
   440→| FR-2.5 | Auto-refresh positions | P1 | Update every 30 seconds |
   441→
   442→### FR-3: Allocation Module
   443→
   444→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   445→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   446→| FR-3.1 | List pending services | P0 | Services needing train assignment |
   447→| FR-3.2 | Generate train recommendations | P0 | Ranked list with scores |
   448→| FR-3.3 | Display justifications | P0 | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
   449→| FR-3.4 | Confirm allocation | P0 | One-click assign |
   450→| FR-3.5 | Filter trains by criteria | P1 | By fleet, depot, km range |
   451→
   452→### FR-4: Balancing Module
   453→
   454→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   455→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   456→| FR-4.1 | Visualize km distribution | P0 | Histogram or box plot |
   457→| FR-4.2 | Rank trains by delta | P0 | Sorted list: most over/under |
   458→| FR-4.3 | Show fleet average | P0 | Reference line on chart |
   459→| FR-4.4 | Identify outliers | P1 | Highlight >15% deviation |
   460→
   461→### FR-5: Simulation Module
   462→
   463→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   464→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   465→| FR-5.1 | Create what-if scenario | P0 | Clone current state to sandbox |
   466→| FR-5.2 | Modify future allocations | P0 | Reassign trains in scenario |
   467→| FR-5.3 | Run simulation | P0 | Calculate projected impact |
   468→| FR-5.4 | Compare scenarios | P0 | Side-by-side metrics |
   469→| FR-5.5 | Apply scenario | P1 | Commit changes to live plan |
   470→
   471→### FR-6: Maintenance Gantt Module
   472→
   473→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   474→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   475→| FR-6.1 | Display timeline view | P1 | 4-week view, trains as rows |
   476→| FR-6.2 | Show maintenance blocks | P1 | Color-coded PM1/PM2/HM |
   477→| FR-6.3 | Enable drag-drop | P1 | Move maintenance to different date |
   478→| FR-6.4 | Validate constraints | P1 | Real-time conflict checking |
   479→| FR-6.5 | Show depot capacity | P1 | Aggregate view per depot/week |
   480→
   481→### FR-7: Recommendation Engine (Solver)
   482→
   483→| ID | Requirement | Priority | Acceptance Criteria |
   484→|----|-------------|----------|---------------------|
   485→| FR-7.1 | Accept service request | P0 | Service ID, date, route profile |
   486→| FR-7.2 | Filter available trains | P0 | Exclude unfit, in-maintenance |
   487→| FR-7.3 | Score by balancing criteria | P0 | Prioritize under-running |
   488→| FR-7.4 | Consider dead running | P1 | Factor proximity to depot |
   489→| FR-7.5 | Generate justifications | P0 | 3+ human-readable reasons |
   490→| FR-7.6 | Return ranked list | P0 | Top 5 recommendations |
   491→
   492→---
   493→
   494→## Non-Functional Requirements
   495→
   496→### NFR-1: Performance
   497→
   498→| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
   499→|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
   500→| NFR-1.1 | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds | Dispatcher patience |
   501→| NFR-1.2 | Screen loading | < 3 seconds | Demo credibility |

   507→

   515→### NFR-3: Reliability
   516→
   517→| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
   518→|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
   519→| NFR-3.1 | Demo stability | 15 min no crash | Presentation length |
   520→| NFR-3.2 | Data consistency | No conflicts | Trust |
   521→| NFR-3.3 | Fallback screens | Available | Risk mitigation |
   522→
   523→### NFR-4: Usability
   524→
   525→| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
   526→|----|-------------|--------|-----------|

   528→| NFR-4.2 | Learning curve | 5 min orientation | Demo scenario |
   529→| NFR-4.3 | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 | Credibility |
   530→
   531→---
   532→
   533→## Data Requirements
   534→
   535→### Core Entities
   536→
   537→#### trains
   538→| Field | Type | Description |
   539→|-------|------|-------------|
   540→| id | UUID | Primary key |
   541→| train_id | VARCHAR(20) | Business identifier (e.g., UTE-3400-012) |
   542→| fleet_type | VARCHAR(50) | Fleet classification |
   543→| base_depot_id | UUID | Home depot FK |

   549→
   550→#### fleets
   551→| Field | Type | Description |
   552→|-------|------|-------------|
   553→| id | UUID | Primary key |
   554→| name | VARCHAR(50) | Fleet name (e.g., Metro Series 2200) |

   559→
   560→#### depots
   561→| Field | Type | Description |
   562→|-------|------|-------------|
   563→| id | UUID | Primary key |
   564→| name | VARCHAR(100) | Depot name |

   567→| longitude | DECIMAL | Location |

   570→
   571→#### services
   572→| Field | Type | Description |

   594→### Seed Data Requirements
   595→
   596→| Entity | Volume | Characteristics |
   597→|--------|--------|-----------------|

   604→
   605→## Integration Requirements
   606→
   607→### API Architecture
   608→
   609→```
   610→[Frontend Next.js]

   616→
   617→### API Endpoints (Demo Scope)
   618→
   619→| Method | Endpoint | Description |
   620→|--------|----------|-------------|

   632→

   634→

   636→```json

   654→      "justifications": [

   656→        "Service ends 5km from base depot",

   659→    }

   665→---
   666→
   667→## Constraints and Dependencies
   668→

   674→| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Seed data, no auth |
   675→| Python solver | Cross-language integration | JSON API |

   677→
   678→### Assumptions
   679→
   680→1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
   681→2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
   682→3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support
   683→4. English UI only (no i18n)
   684→5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)
   685→
   686→### Dependencies
   687→
   688→| Dependency | Version | Purpose |
   689→|------------|---------|---------|

   699→| Mapbox/Leaflet | latest | Map visualization |
   700→| Recharts | 2.x | Charts |
   701→
   702→---
   703→
   704→## Appendix: Domain Glossary
   705→
   706→| Term | Definition |
   707→|------|------------|
   708→| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles (trains, carriages) requiring maintenance |
   709→| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile |
   710→| Base Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet |
   711→| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) |
   712→| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes |
   713→| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet |
   714→| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations |
   715→| PM1/PM2/HM | Preventive Maintenance levels (light/medium/heavy) |
   716→| EAM | Enterprise Asset Management - external system |
   717→| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle |
   718→| Overrun | Train exceeds target mileage |
   719→| Underrun | Train falls short of target mileage |
   720→| Variance | Measure of mileage distribution spread across fleet |
