# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System - Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for MMS, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design, and Architecture into implementable stories organized by user value delivery.

**Project Context:**
- 12-week demo timeline
- 15-minute presentation to win competitive bid
- Primary differentiator: Km balancing as primary objective with visible justifications

---

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Module | Requirement | Priority |
|----|--------|-------------|----------|
| FR-1.1 | Dashboard | Display fleet summary KPIs (total, available, maintenance, variance) | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Dashboard | Show variance indicator with traffic-light (≤10% green, 10-20% yellow, >20% red) | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Dashboard | Display depot capacity cards | P1 |
| FR-1.4 | Dashboard | Show maintenance alerts (upcoming PM/HM in 7 days) | P1 |
| FR-1.5 | Dashboard | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 |
| FR-2.1 | Map | Display train positions on map | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | Map | Color-code markers by status (available=green, in-service=blue, maintenance=red) | P0 |
| FR-2.3 | Map | Show depot locations with capacity indicator | P1 |
| FR-2.4 | Map | Enable train selection (click marker shows train card) | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Map | Auto-refresh positions every 30 seconds | P1 |
| FR-3.1 | Allocation | List pending services | P0 |
| FR-3.2 | Allocation | Generate train recommendations (ranked list with scores) | P0 |
| FR-3.3 | Allocation | Display justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation) | P0 |
| FR-3.4 | Allocation | Confirm allocation (one-click assign) | P0 |
| FR-3.5 | Allocation | Filter trains by criteria (fleet, depot, km range) | P1 |
| FR-4.1 | Balancing | Visualize km distribution (histogram or box plot) | P0 |
| FR-4.2 | Balancing | Rank trains by delta (sorted list: most over/under) | P0 |
| FR-4.3 | Balancing | Show fleet average reference line | P0 |
| FR-4.4 | Balancing | Identify outliers (highlight >15% deviation) | P1 |
| FR-5.1 | Simulation | Create what-if scenario (clone current state) | P0 |
| FR-5.2 | Simulation | Modify future allocations in scenario | P0 |
| FR-5.3 | Simulation | Run simulation (calculate projected impact) | P0 |
| FR-5.4 | Simulation | Compare scenarios side-by-side | P0 |
| FR-5.5 | Simulation | Apply scenario to live plan | P1 |
| FR-6.1 | Gantt | Display timeline view (4-week, trains as rows) | P1 |
| FR-6.2 | Gantt | Show maintenance blocks (color-coded PM1/PM2/HM) | P1 |
| FR-6.3 | Gantt | Enable drag-drop scheduling | P1 |
| FR-6.4 | Gantt | Validate constraints real-time | P1 |
| FR-6.5 | Gantt | Show depot capacity per week | P1 |
| FR-7.1 | Solver | Accept service request (ID, date, route profile) | P0 |
| FR-7.2 | Solver | Filter available trains (exclude unfit, in-maintenance) | P0 |
| FR-7.3 | Solver | Score by balancing criteria (prioritize under-running) | P0 |
| FR-7.4 | Solver | Consider dead running (factor proximity) | P1 |
| FR-7.5 | Solver | Generate justifications (3+ human-readable reasons) | P0 |
| FR-7.6 | Solver | Return ranked list (top 5 recommendations) | P0 |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Category | Requirement | Target |
|----|----------|-------------|--------|
| NFR-1.1 | Performance | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | Performance | Screen loading | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.3 | Performance | Map refresh | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Performance | Simulation response | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | Performance | Database queries | < 500ms |
| NFR-2.1 | Scalability | Trains supported | 80-120 |
| NFR-2.2 | Scalability | Depots supported | 12-15 |
| NFR-2.3 | Scalability | Daily services | 200-300 |
| NFR-2.4 | Scalability | Concurrent users | 10-20 |
| NFR-3.1 | Reliability | Demo stability | 15 min no crash |
| NFR-3.2 | Reliability | Data consistency | No conflicts |
| NFR-3.3 | Reliability | Fallback screens | Available |
| NFR-4.1 | Usability | Allocation flow | < 2 min, < 5 clicks |
| NFR-4.2 | Usability | Learning curve | 5 min orientation |
| NFR-4.3 | Usability | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 |

### Additional Requirements (from Architecture)

| ID | Category | Requirement |
|----|----------|-------------|
| AR-1 | Infrastructure | Docker Compose development environment |
| AR-2 | Infrastructure | Monorepo structure (apps/web, apps/api, solver/) |
| AR-3 | Database | PostgreSQL 16 with defined schema |
| AR-4 | Database | Seed data (100-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services) |
| AR-5 | Backend | Fastify API server with routes per module |
| AR-6 | Backend | Redis caching with defined TTLs |
| AR-7 | Solver | Python FastAPI with OR-Tools CP-SAT |
| AR-8 | Frontend | Next.js 14 App Router structure |
| AR-9 | Frontend | @zazcart/toolkit for state management |
| AR-10 | Frontend | shadcn/ui component library |
| AR-11 | Resilience | Solver fallback (simple scoring if timeout) |
| AR-12 | Resilience | Error boundaries with retry |

---

### FR Coverage Map

| Epic | Functional Requirements Covered | NFRs Addressed |
|------|--------------------------------|----------------|
| Epic 1 | AR-1 to AR-12 | NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3 |
| Epic 2 | FR-1.1, FR-1.2, FR-1.3, FR-1.4, FR-1.5 | NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3 |
| Epic 3 | FR-2.1, FR-2.2, FR-2.3, FR-2.4, FR-2.5 | NFR-1.3 |
| Epic 4 | FR-3.1 to FR-3.5, FR-7.1 to FR-7.6 | NFR-1.1, NFR-4.1 |
| Epic 5 | FR-4.1 to FR-4.4, FR-5.1 to FR-5.5 | NFR-1.4 |
| Epic 6 | FR-6.1 to FR-6.5 | NFR-4.2 |

---

## Epic List

| Epic | Title | Primary Persona | Priority | Stories |
|------|-------|-----------------|----------|---------|
| 1 | Project Foundation & Infrastructure | Developer | P0 | 6 |
| 2 | Dashboard & KPIs | Gabriel (Manager) | P0 | 5 |
| 3 | Map & Fleet Visualization | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 5 |
| 4 | Allocation with Recommendations | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 6 |
| 5 | Km Balancing & Simulation | Paul (Planner) | P0 | 6 |
| 6 | Maintenance Gantt | Paul (Planner) | P1 | 5 |

---

## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Infrastructure

**Goal:** Establish the technical foundation enabling all feature development, including monorepo structure, database schema, API skeleton, and Python solver service.

**Covers:** AR-1 to AR-12, NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3

---

### Story 1.1: Setup Monorepo Structure

As a **developer**,
I want **a properly structured monorepo with all required packages**,
So that **I can develop frontend, backend, and solver in a coordinated way**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty project directory
**When** the scaffolding script completes
**Then** the following structure exists:
```
mms/
├── package.json (workspaces config)
├── apps/web/ (Next.js 14)
├── apps/api/ (Fastify)
├── packages/types/ (shared TypeScript)
├── solver/ (Python)
└── docker-compose.yml
```
**And** `bun install` succeeds without errors
**And** TypeScript compilation passes for all packages

---

### Story 1.2: Configure Database Schema

As a **developer**,
I want **the complete PostgreSQL schema with migrations**,
So that **all entities are properly modeled and queryable**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** PostgreSQL 16 is running via Docker
**When** migrations are applied
**Then** the following tables exist with correct relationships:
- `fleets` (id, name, manufacturer, pm1/pm2/hm intervals)
- `depots` (id, name, code, lat/lon, capacity_tracks, capacity_manhours)
- `trains` (id, train_id, fleet_id FK, base_depot_id FK, current_km, fitness_status)
- `services` (id, service_code, origin, destination, distance_km, times)
- `allocations` (id, service_id FK, train_id FK, date, status, justifications JSONB)
- `mileage_history` (id, train_id FK, recorded_date, km_value)
- `maintenance_schedules` (id, train_id FK, depot_id FK, type, date, status)
**And** all indexes are created for performance
**And** FK constraints enforce data integrity

---

### Story 1.3: Create Seed Data Generator

As a **developer**,
I want **realistic seed data for demos**,
So that **the system demonstrates with believable railway data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty database with schema applied
**When** the seed script runs
**Then** the database contains:
- 4-5 fleets (e.g., Metro Series 2200, Intercity Series 3400)
- 12-15 depots with Portuguese city names and coordinates
- 100-120 trains distributed across fleets with varied mileage (±30% from average)
- 200-300 daily services with realistic routes
- 6 months of mileage history showing km progression
**And** km variance across fleet is between 15-20% (to demonstrate balancing need)
**And** 5-10 trains are marked as outliers (>25% deviation)

---

### Story 1.4: Implement Fastify API Skeleton

As a **developer**,
I want **a working Fastify server with route stubs**,
So that **frontend can start integration early**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the API server starts successfully
**When** health check endpoint is called
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO date }`
**And** the following route files exist with stub implementations:
- `/api/dashboard/*` (summary, depot-capacity)
- `/api/trains/*` (list, details, history)
- `/api/depots/*` (list)
- `/api/services/*` (pending)
- `/api/allocations/*` (recommend, create)
- `/api/simulation/*` (create, run, compare)
**And** each stub returns appropriate mock data
**And** CORS is configured for `localhost:3000`

---

### Story 1.5: Setup Python Solver Service

As a **developer**,
I want **a FastAPI solver service with OR-Tools installed**,
So that **recommendation engine development can begin**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Python 3.11 environment with requirements installed
**When** the solver service starts
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', ortools_version: 'x.x' }`
**And** `POST /recommend` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** `POST /simulate` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** response includes `solver_time_ms` field
**And** service runs on port 8000

---

### Story 1.6: Configure Docker Compose Environment

As a **developer**,
I want **one-command local development startup**,
So that **I can quickly reset and run the complete system**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Docker and Docker Compose are installed
**When** `docker-compose up` is executed
**Then** the following services start:
- PostgreSQL 16 on port 5432
- Redis 7 on port 6379
**And** database is initialized with schema
**And** `docker-compose down -v && docker-compose up` resets to clean state
**And** `bun run seed` populates demo data after services are up

---

## Epic 2: Dashboard & KPIs

**Goal:** Provide Gabriel (Fleet Manager) with an executive overview showing fleet health, mileage variance, and actionable alerts at a glance.

**Covers:** FR-1.1 to FR-1.5, NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3

**Primary Persona:** Gabriel (Fleet Manager)

---

### Story 2.1: Display Fleet Summary KPIs

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see total trains, available count, maintenance count, and km variance at a glance**,
So that **I understand fleet health in seconds**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/dashboard/summary`
**Then** four KPI cards are displayed:
- Total Trains: shows count (e.g., "112")
- Available: shows count and percentage (e.g., "98 (87%)")
- In Maintenance: shows count (e.g., "14")
- Km Variance: shows percentage (e.g., "18%")
**And** each card uses EN 15380 terminology (not "Vehicle" or "Asset")
**And** page loads in < 3 seconds (NFR-1.2)

---

### Story 2.2: Show Variance Traffic-Light Indicator

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **the variance KPI to show green/yellow/red status**,
So that **I immediately know if mileage distribution is healthy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard displays the Km Variance KPI
**When** variance is ≤10%
**Then** the indicator shows green color and checkmark icon
**When** variance is between 10-20%
**Then** the indicator shows yellow/amber color and warning icon
**When** variance is >20%
**Then** the indicator shows red color and alert icon
**And** hovering shows tooltip: "Fleet mileage variance: X% (target: ≤10%)"

---

### Story 2.3: Display Depot Capacity Cards

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see each depot's current occupancy**,
So that **I can identify capacity bottlenecks**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity`
**Then** depot cards are displayed in a grid (2-4 columns)
**And** each card shows:
- Depot name and code
- Occupancy bar (current/max tracks)
- Percentage used with color coding (green <70%, yellow 70-90%, red >90%)
**And** clicking a depot card navigates to depot detail view

---

### Story 2.4: Show Maintenance Alerts

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see upcoming maintenance events**,
So that **I'm aware of planned capacity reductions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** there are maintenance events scheduled in the next 7 days
**Then** an alerts panel shows:
- Count of upcoming PM1, PM2, HM events
- List of next 5 events with train ID, type, date, depot
**And** clicking an alert navigates to the Maintenance Gantt view
**And** if no upcoming maintenance, shows "No scheduled maintenance in next 7 days"

---

### Story 2.5: Enable Drill-Down Navigation

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to click any element to see details**,
So that **I can investigate issues without navigating menus**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard is displayed
**When** I click on "Available" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by status=fit
**When** I click on "In Maintenance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Maintenance Gantt view
**When** I click on "Km Variance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Balancing view with distribution chart
**When** I click on a depot card
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by depot
**And** all navigation maintains context (no page reload flash)

---

## Epic 3: Map & Fleet Visualization

**Goal:** Provide Diana (Dispatcher) with a real-time visual map showing train positions, depot locations, and quick access to train details.

**Covers:** FR-2.1 to FR-2.5, NFR-1.3

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 3.1: Display Train Positions on Map

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all active trains on an interactive map**,
So that **I understand current fleet distribution**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** train position data fetches from `/api/trains`
**Then** a map centered on Portugal displays
**And** each train appears as a marker at its lat/lon position
**And** trains without position show at their base depot location
**And** map renders in < 2 seconds

---

### Story 3.2: Color-Code Markers by Status

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **trains color-coded by operational status**,
So that **I quickly identify available trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** a train has fitness_status='fit' and no active service
**Then** marker is green (Available)
**When** a train has an active allocation today
**Then** marker is blue (In-Service)
**When** a train has fitness_status='unfit' or scheduled maintenance
**Then** marker is red (Maintenance)
**And** a legend shows the three status colors
**And** filter toggles allow showing/hiding each status

---

### Story 3.3: Show Depot Locations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **depot locations visible on the map**,
So that **I understand the maintenance network**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/depots`
**Then** each depot appears as a distinct icon (not train marker)
**And** depot icon shows capacity usage (color-coded pie or bar)
**And** hovering depot shows tooltip: "Name | Tracks: X/Y | Manhours: Z available"
**And** depots can be toggled on/off via map controls

---

### Story 3.4: Enable Train Selection

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to click a train marker and see details**,
So that **I can assess train suitability quickly**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** I click a train marker
**Then** a slide-out panel or popup card shows:
- Train ID (e.g., UTE-3400-012)
- Fleet type
- Current km and variance from average
- Fitness status
- Base depot
- Next scheduled maintenance (type, date, km)
**And** card includes "View Details" link to full train page
**And** card includes "Allocate" button (if train is available)

---

### Story 3.5: Auto-Refresh Positions

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **train positions to update automatically**,
So that **I see near-real-time fleet state**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page is open
**When** 30 seconds pass
**Then** train positions refresh automatically
**And** markers animate smoothly to new positions (no flicker)
**And** a "Last updated: HH:MM:SS" indicator shows refresh time
**And** manual "Refresh Now" button is available
**And** refresh completes in < 5 seconds (NFR-1.3)

---

## Epic 4: Allocation with Recommendations

**Goal:** Enable Diana (Dispatcher) to allocate trains to services efficiently using AI-powered recommendations with visible justifications.

**Covers:** FR-3.1 to FR-3.5, FR-7.1 to FR-7.6, NFR-1.1, NFR-4.1

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 4.1: List Pending Services

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all services needing train assignment**,
So that **I can prioritize my allocation work**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Allocation page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/services/pending`
**Then** a list/table shows pending services with columns:
- Service Code (e.g., IC 501)
- Origin → Destination
- Distance (km)
- Departure time
- Status (Unassigned/Assigned)
**And** list is sorted by departure time ascending
**And** I can filter by date using a date picker
**And** clicking a service selects it for recommendation

---

### Story 4.2: Generate Train Recommendations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **the system to recommend suitable trains ranked by score**,
So that **I make better allocation decisions faster**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have selected a pending service
**When** I click "Recommend"
**Then** the system calls `/api/allocations/recommend`
**And** within 30 seconds (NFR-1.1), a ranked list of 5 trains appears
**And** each recommendation shows:
- Train ID
- Score (0-100)
- Km delta from average (e.g., "-12%")
- Dead running distance
**And** recommendations are sorted by score descending
**And** if solver times out, fallback simple ranking is shown with notice

---

### Story 4.3: Display Justifications

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see why each train is recommended**,
So that **I trust the system's suggestions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** recommendations are displayed
**When** I expand or hover a recommendation
**Then** I see 3+ justification reasons, such as:
- "Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)"
- "Service ends 5km from Porto depot"
- "PM1 scheduled in 3 days at destination depot"
- "Route compatible with train aptitude"
**And** each justification is a complete, human-readable sentence
**And** justifications use EN 15380 terminology

---

### Story 4.4: Confirm Allocation

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to assign a recommended train with one click**,
So that **I complete allocations efficiently**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I see the recommended trains list
**When** I click "Assign" on a recommendation
**Then** the system calls `POST /api/allocations`
**And** success toast shows "Train [ID] assigned to [Service]"
**And** the service moves from Pending to Assigned list
**And** the train's status updates on the Map
**And** entire flow completes in < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks (NFR-4.1)

---

### Story 4.5: Filter Trains by Criteria

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to filter available trains by fleet, depot, or km range**,
So that **I can find specific trains when needed**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Allocation page
**When** I open the filter panel
**Then** I can filter by:
- Fleet type (multi-select dropdown)
- Base depot (multi-select dropdown)
- Km range (min/max slider or inputs)
- Fitness status (fit only / show all)
**And** filters apply immediately to train list
**And** active filters are shown as chips that can be removed

---

### Story 4.6: Implement Solver Recommendation Engine

As a **system**,
I want **the Python solver to calculate optimal train assignments**,
So that **recommendations maximize mileage balancing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a `POST /recommend` request with service and candidates
**When** the OR-Tools CP-SAT solver runs
**Then** the response includes:
- Top 5 ranked trains by optimization score
- 3+ justification strings per train
- Dead running km calculation
- Solver execution time in ms
**And** solver respects constraints:
1. Exclude unfit trains (hard constraint)
2. Exclude trains with overlapping allocations (hard constraint)
3. Prioritize under-running trains (soft constraint, weight: high)
4. Minimize dead running (soft constraint, weight: medium)
5. Prefer trains ending near maintenance depot (soft constraint, weight: low)
**And** solver completes within 30 seconds or returns partial results

---

## Epic 5: Km Balancing & Simulation

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize fleet mileage distribution, identify imbalances, and simulate allocation strategies to optimize balancing.

**Covers:** FR-4.1 to FR-4.4, FR-5.1 to FR-5.5, NFR-1.4

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 5.1: Visualize Km Distribution

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see the km distribution across the fleet as a chart**,
So that **I understand the mileage spread**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** distribution data fetches from `/api/balancing/distribution`
**Then** a histogram or box plot displays showing:
- X-axis: km ranges (buckets)
- Y-axis: number of trains
- Fleet average line clearly marked
- Target range highlighted (±10% of average)
**And** chart is interactive (hover shows train count per bucket)
**And** I can filter by fleet type

---

### Story 5.2: Rank Trains by Delta

As a **Planner**,
I want **a sorted list showing trains by km deviation**,
So that **I quickly identify over/under-running trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** train ranking is displayed
**Then** a sortable table shows:
- Train ID
- Current km
- Fleet average km
- Delta (km and %)
- Status indicator (over/under/balanced)
**And** default sort is by absolute delta descending (worst first)
**And** clicking a train row shows details or navigates to train page
**And** top 5 over-running and top 5 under-running are highlighted

---

### Story 5.3: Identify Outliers

As a **Planner**,
I want **outlier trains visually highlighted**,
So that **I focus attention on problem trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the distribution chart is displayed
**When** trains deviate >15% from fleet average
**Then** their histogram bar or point is highlighted in distinct color
**And** outlier trains are listed in a separate "Outliers" panel
**And** each outlier shows delta and recommended action:
- Over-running: "Reduce high-km allocations"
- Under-running: "Prioritize for upcoming services"

---

### Story 5.4: Create What-If Scenario

As a **Planner**,
I want **to create a simulation scenario**,
So that **I can test allocation strategies without affecting live data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Simulation section
**When** I click "New Scenario"
**Then** a dialog asks for scenario name
**And** clicking "Create" calls `POST /api/simulation/create`
**And** a sandbox copy of current allocations is created
**And** I enter "scenario mode" with visual indicator
**And** changes in scenario mode don't affect production data

---

### Story 5.5: Modify and Run Simulation

As a **Planner**,
I want **to modify allocations in a scenario and see projected impact**,
So that **I can evaluate strategies before committing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am in scenario mode
**When** I reassign trains to different services
**Then** changes are tracked in the scenario
**And** clicking "Run Simulation" calls `POST /api/simulation/:id/run`
**And** within 10 seconds (NFR-1.4), results show:
- Projected variance after 30 days
- Projected dead running total
- Change from current state (better/worse indicators)
**And** I can run multiple iterations with different changes

---

### Story 5.6: Compare Scenarios

As a **Planner**,
I want **to compare scenarios side-by-side**,
So that **I choose the best strategy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have created multiple scenarios
**When** I select 2-3 scenarios to compare
**Then** a comparison view shows:
- Metrics in columns (variance, dead running, cost impact)
- Best value highlighted green in each row
- Chart overlay showing distribution difference
**And** I can select a scenario and click "Apply to Live"
**And** applying scenario updates the production allocation plan

---

## Epic 6: Maintenance Gantt

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize and schedule preventive maintenance using an interactive Gantt timeline with conflict validation.

**Covers:** FR-6.1 to FR-6.5, NFR-4.2

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 6.1: Display Timeline View

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see maintenance schedules on a Gantt timeline**,
So that **I understand the maintenance calendar**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Schedule/Gantt page loads
**When** maintenance data fetches
**Then** a Gantt chart displays:
- Y-axis: Train IDs (rows)
- X-axis: 4-week timeline (days as columns)
- Horizontal scroll for extended view
- Today line clearly marked
**And** I can zoom in/out (week view, 2-week view, 4-week view)
**And** I can filter trains by fleet or depot

---

### Story 6.2: Show Maintenance Blocks

As a **Planner**,
I want **maintenance events as color-coded blocks**,
So that **I distinguish maintenance types**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt timeline is displayed
**When** trains have scheduled maintenance
**Then** blocks appear on the timeline:
- PM1: Light blue
- PM2: Dark blue
- HM: Red
- Overhaul: Purple
**And** block width corresponds to duration (days)
**And** hovering block shows:
- Maintenance type
- Scheduled date(s)
- Depot
- Km at maintenance
**And** legend shows color meanings

---

### Story 6.3: Enable Drag-Drop Scheduling

As a **Planner**,
I want **to drag maintenance blocks to different dates**,
So that **I can adjust schedules interactively**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt displays maintenance blocks
**When** I drag a maintenance block to a new date
**Then** the block moves visually following cursor
**And** drop zones highlight valid/invalid dates
**And** dropping on valid date updates the schedule
**And** system calls API to persist change
**And** undo option available for 10 seconds after change

---

### Story 6.4: Validate Constraints Real-Time

As a **Planner**,
I want **immediate feedback if a schedule change violates constraints**,
So that **I avoid creating invalid schedules**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am dragging a maintenance block
**When** I hover over a date that would cause conflict
**Then** the drop zone shows red indicator
**And** tooltip explains the conflict:
- "Depot at capacity on this date"
- "Outside maintenance window tolerance"
- "Overlaps with another maintenance"
**And** if I drop on invalid date, change is rejected with explanation
**And** valid changes show green confirmation

---

### Story 6.5: Show Depot Capacity per Week

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see aggregate depot capacity usage**,
So that **I avoid overloading maintenance facilities**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt page loads
**When** I view the capacity panel
**Then** a heatmap or bar chart shows:
- Each depot as a row
- Each week as a column
- Color intensity indicates utilization (green < 70%, yellow 70-90%, red > 90%)
**And** clicking a cell shows trains scheduled at that depot that week
**And** if any depot/week is at 100%, warning icon appears

---

# NEW EPICS - Added via SCP-001 (2025-12-28)

## Epic 7: What-If Scenario Planning System

**Priority:** Critical
**Dependencies:** Epic 5 (Balancing foundation)
**Added:** SCP-001 - Visual testing revealed What-If is non-functional stub

### Description

Complete scenario planning system allowing planners to create sandbox environments, test different allocation strategies, simulate workforce changes, and compare outcomes before committing to production.

### Stories

#### Story 7.1: Scenario Sandbox Mode
**ID:** 7-1-scenario-sandbox-mode
**Priority:** P0

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** create a sandbox copy of current fleet state
**So that** I can experiment with changes without affecting live data

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] "Create Scenario" button clones current state
- [ ] Sandbox is visually distinguished (badge/border)
- [ ] Changes in sandbox don't affect live data
- [ ] Can discard sandbox anytime
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.2: Fleet Size Simulation
**ID:** 7-2-fleet-size-simulation
**Priority:** P1

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** add/remove trains from a scenario
**So that** I can see impact on km distribution and variance

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can add virtual trains to scenario
- [ ] Can remove trains from scenario
- [ ] KPIs update in real-time
- [ ] Impact on variance displayed
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.3: Workforce/Vacation Planning
**ID:** 7-3-workforce-vacation-planning
**Priority:** P1

**As a** maintenance manager
**I want to** simulate staff absences
**So that** I can test coverage during holidays

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can mark dates as reduced capacity
- [ ] Depot capacity reflects reductions
- [ ] Conflict warnings if over-scheduled
- [ ] Calendar visualization
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.4: Budget Allocation Scenarios
**ID:** 7-4-budget-allocation-scenarios
**Priority:** P2

**As a** fleet manager
**I want to** test different budget distributions
**So that** I can optimize maintenance spending

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can set budget per depot/period
- [ ] Shows maintenance coverage at budget
- [ ] Highlights unfunded maintenance
- [ ] ROI estimates displayed
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.5: Scenario Comparison Dashboard
**ID:** 7-5-scenario-comparison-dashboard
**Priority:** P0

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** compare 2-3 scenarios side-by-side
**So that** I can choose the best strategy

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Select multiple scenarios for comparison
- [ ] Side-by-side KPI display
- [ ] Best/worst values highlighted (green/red)
- [ ] Delta percentages shown
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.6: Apply Scenario to Live
**ID:** 7-6-apply-scenario-to-live
**Priority:** P1

**As a** fleet manager
**I want to** commit an approved scenario
**So that** changes become the new baseline

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] "Apply to Live" button with confirmation
- [ ] Audit trail of changes created
- [ ] Rollback capability within 24h
- [ ] Notification to stakeholders
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

## Epic 8: Visual Redesign & Canvas Migration

**Priority:** Critical
**Dependencies:** None (foundation work)
**Added:** SCP-001 - Visual testing revealed canvas broken and design inconsistent

### Description

Unify the design system with a single source of truth, fix all dark/light mode inconsistencies, and migrate from broken Pixi.js canvas to deck.gl with proper semantic zoom.

### Stories

#### Story 8.1: Unified Design Tokens
**ID:** 8-1-unified-design-tokens
**Priority:** P0

**As a** developer
**I want** a single source of truth for colors and spacing
**So that** all components are visually consistent

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] CSS variables defined in globals.css
- [ ] Tailwind config uses same palette
- [ ] All components use tokens, not hardcoded colors
- [ ] Design token documentation created
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification all pages**

---

#### Story 8.2: Typography and Terminology
**ID:** 8-2-typography-and-terminology
**Priority:** P1

**As a** user
**I want** consistent font sizes and railway terminology
**So that** the interface is professional

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Font scale defined (xs to 3xl)
- [ ] Railway terminology standardized (PM1/PM2/HM/OVH)
- [ ] Labels use correct terminology
- [ ] Typography documentation created
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 8.3: Component Dark Mode Fix
**ID:** 8-3-component-dark-mode-fix
**Priority:** P0

**As a** user
**I want** all components in dark mode
**So that** the interface is consistent

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] MaintenanceGantt.tsx uses dark background
- [ ] Layout.tsx uses dark background
- [ ] No white/light backgrounds anywhere
- [ ] All text has proper contrast
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright screenshots all pages**

---

#### Story 8.4: deck.gl Canvas Migration
**ID:** 8-4-deck-gl-canvas-migration
**Priority:** P0

**As a** developer
**I want to** replace Pixi.js with deck.gl
**So that** canvas rendering works properly

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Schedule canvas renders train blocks
- [ ] Pan and zoom work smoothly
- [ ] Performance acceptable (60fps)
- [ ] WebGL2 backend configured
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 8.5: Semantic Zoom LOD
**ID:** 8-5-semantic-zoom-lod
**Priority:** P1

**As a** user
**I want** different detail levels at different zoom scales
**So that** I see appropriate information

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] MACRO level (6+ months): Fleet summary only
- [ ] MESO level (1-6 months): Individual trains, grouped events
- [ ] MICRO level (<1 month): Full detail with times
- [ ] Smooth transitions between levels
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright zoom interaction tests**

---

#### Story 8.6: Interaction Testing Suite
**ID:** 8-6-interaction-testing-suite
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want** automated interaction tests
**So that** all user actions are verified

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Pan tests (drag canvas)
- [ ] Zoom tests (scroll wheel, pinch)
- [ ] Click tests (all buttons, links)
- [ ] Drag-drop tests (schedule editing)
- [ ] Form input tests (what-if parameters)
- [ ] Tests run in CI pipeline

---

## Epic 9: Quality Assurance Sprint

**Priority:** High
**Dependencies:** None
**Added:** SCP-001 - Verify all existing stories were properly implemented

### Description

Systematic audit of all previously "completed" stories using Playwright MCP visual verification. Each story must have screenshot evidence proving it works.

### Stories

#### Story 9.1: Audit Epic 1 Stories
**ID:** 9-1-audit-epic-1-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 1 stories
**So that** foundation is confirmed working

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each story tested with Playwright
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each
- [ ] Test log created
- [ ] Issues documented

---

#### Story 9.2: Audit Epic 2 Stories
**ID:** 9-2-audit-epic-2-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 2 stories
**So that** Dashboard is confirmed working

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each KPI verified on screen
- [ ] Drill-down navigation tested
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each
- [ ] Issues documented

---

#### Story 9.3: Audit Epic 3 Stories
**ID:** 9-3-audit-epic-3-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 3 stories
**So that** Map and Fleet pages work

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Map renders with markers
- [ ] Fleet page shows train list (NOT stub)
- [ ] Train selection works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.4: Audit Epic 4 Stories
**ID:** 9-4-audit-epic-4-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 4 stories
**So that** Allocation works

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Services show times (not "--:--")
- [ ] Recommendations appear when service selected
- [ ] Allocation confirmation works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.5: Audit Epic 5 Stories
**ID:** 9-5-audit-epic-5-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 5 stories
**So that** Balancing and What-If work

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Histogram renders correctly
- [ ] What-If creates actual scenarios
- [ ] Scenarios can be modified
- [ ] Comparison works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.6: Audit Epic 6 Stories
**ID:** 9-6-audit-epic-6-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 6 stories
**So that** Maintenance Gantt works

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Canvas shows maintenance blocks
- [ ] Drag-drop scheduling works
- [ ] Depot capacity accurate
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

## Appendix: Story Dependencies

```
Epic 1 (Foundation)
   └── All other epics depend on Epic 1 completion

Epic 2 (Dashboard) ─────┐
                        ├── Epic 4 (Allocation) uses train data
Epic 3 (Map) ──────────┘

Epic 4 (Allocation)
   └── Requires Solver (Story 4.6)

Epic 5 (Simulation)
   └── Requires Balancing data (depends on Epic 4)

Epic 6 (Gantt)
   └── Independent after Epic 1

# NEW DEPENDENCIES (SCP-001)

Epic 7 (What-If)
   └── Requires Epic 5 foundation (Balancing KPIs)

Epic 8 (Visual Redesign)
   └── Foundation work, no dependencies
   └── Should be done before Epic 6 fixes (canvas)

Epic 9 (QA Audit)
   └── No dependencies, runs in parallel
```

---

## Sprint Recommendation

### Original Plan (Completed - Needs Verification)

| Sprint | Epics | Focus | Status |
|--------|-------|-------|--------|
| Sprint 1 | Epic 1 (all), Epic 2 (2.1-2.2) | Foundation + Dashboard core | DONE |
| Sprint 2 | Epic 2 (2.3-2.5), Epic 3 | Dashboard complete + Map | PARTIAL |
| Sprint 3 | Epic 4 (all) | Allocation + Solver | PARTIAL |
| Sprint 4 | Epic 5 (all) | Balancing + Simulation | PARTIAL |
| Sprint 5 | Epic 6 (all) | Gantt | BROKEN |
| Sprint 6 | Integration, Polish, Demo prep | End-to-end testing | NOT DONE |

### Updated Plan (SCP-001)

| Sprint | Epics | Focus | Priority |
|--------|-------|-------|----------|
| Recovery 1 | Epic 8 (8.1-8.3), Epic 9 (all) | Design tokens + QA audit | CRITICAL |
| Recovery 2 | Epic 8 (8.4-8.6) | Canvas fix + deck.gl | CRITICAL |
| Recovery 3 | Epic 6 fixes, Epic 3 fixes | Fix broken stories | HIGH |
| Recovery 4 | Epic 7 (all) | What-If scenarios | HIGH |
| Recovery 5 | Integration, Demo prep | Final polish | MEDIUM |

---

content.content:
1→# Professional Profiles for MMS Project
     2→
     3→> Requirements document for hiring specialists to review the MMS Demo Playbook and work on the project.
     4→>
     5→> **Prepared by:** Product Owner with 15 years of experience in railway systems
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Railway Domain Specialists](#1-railway-domain-specialists)
    12→2. [Technical Specialists](#2-technical-specialists)
    13→3. [Product and UX Specialists](#3-product-and-ux-specialists)
    14→4. [Quality and Delivery Specialists](#4-quality-and-delivery-specialists)
    15→5. [Business and Presentation Specialists](#5-business-and-presentation-specialists)
    16→6. [Summary and Prioritization](#6-summary-and-prioritization)
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## 1. Railway Domain Specialists
    21→
    22→### 1.1 Rolling Stock Engineer
    23→
    24→#### Mandatory Experience
    25→
    26→| Requirement | Minimum |
    27→|-------------|---------|
    28→| Time in rolling stock maintenance | 8 years |
    29→| Maintenance cycles (PM1, PM2, HM, Overhaul) | Full mastery |
    30→| Wheelset management | Reprofiling, flange limits |
    31→| Standards | EN 15380, ECM regulation |
    32→
    33→#### Specific Knowledge
    34→
    35→- Maintenance intervals by train type (EMU, DMU, locomotives)
    36→- Critical component degradation (bogie, wheelset, pantograph)
    37→- Wayside detection systems (TBOGI, hot axle detectors)
    38→- Fleet lifecycle and TCO calculation
    39→- Differences between manufacturers (Alstom, Siemens, Stadler, CAF)
    40→
    41→#### Project Responsibilities
    42→
    43→| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
    44→|-------|----------|------------|
    45→| Review | Validate Section 2 (Domain) — intervals, terminology | 8h |
    46→| Review | Validate Section 9 (Seed Data) — number realism | 4h |
    47→| Review | Complete Appendix A (Glossary) | 4h |
    48→| Validation | Review demo before presentation | 4h |
    49→
    50→**Total estimated:** 20 hours
    51→
    52→#### Nice-to-Have
    53→
    54→- ECM (Entity in Charge of Maintenance) certification
    55→- Experience with multiple manufacturers
    56→- Work at large-scale operator (200+ trains)
    57→
    58→---
    59→
    60→### 1.2 Railway Operations Planner
    61→
    62→#### Mandatory Experience
    63→
    64→| Requirement | Minimum |
    65→|-------------|---------|
    66→| Time in railway operations planning | 10 years |
    67→| Rolling stock diagrams | Creation and optimization |
    68→| Crew scheduling | Integration with rolling stock |
    69→| Disruption management | Practical experience |
    70→
    71→#### Specific Knowledge
    72→
    73→- Rolling stock diagram creation
    74→- Crew constraints:
    75→  - Route knowledge
    76→  - Traction knowledge (certification by train type)
    77→  - Hours of Service (legal working hour limits)
    78→- Dead running optimization
    79→- Depot capacity management
    80→- Often ignored operational times:
    81→  - Sign-on: 15-30 min
    82→  - Walk time: 5-15 min
    83→  - Preparation: 10-20 min
    84→  - Securing: 5-15 min
    85→- Shunting — time and cost
    86→
    87→#### Project Responsibilities
    88→
    89→| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
    90→|-------|----------|------------|
    91→| Review | Validate Section 2.4 (Unknown Unknowns) | 8h |
    92→| Review | Validate Section 5 (Functional Requirements) | 6h |
    93→| Review | Validate Section 8.4 (Solver) — constraints | 8h |
    94→| Validation | Test flows from operator perspective | 8h |
    95→| Demo | Review script and FAQ | 6h |
    96→| Support | Available for questions during sprints | 4h |
    97→
    98→**Total estimated:** 40 hours
    99→
   100→#### Nice-to-Have
   101→
   102→- Experience with DELMIA Quintiq or Tracsis
   103→- Work at large-scale operator (500+ trains)
   104→- Multi-modal experience (passenger + freight)
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→### 1.3 Fleet Manager
   109→
   110→#### Mandatory Experience
   111→
   112→| Requirement | Minimum |
   113→|-------------|---------|
   114→| Time in rolling stock fleet management | 7 years |
   115→| Availability and utilization KPIs | Direct responsibility |
   116→| Mileage balancing | Practical implementation |
   117→| Maintenance contracts | TSSSA, PBC, or similar |
   118→
   119→#### Specific Knowledge
   120→
   121→- Fleet metrics:
   122→  - MDBF (Mean Distance Between Failures)
   123→  - MTTR (Mean Time To Repair)
   124→  - Availability rate
   125→  - Fleet utilization
   126→- Wear balancing strategies
   127→- Trade-offs between utilization and maintenance
   128→- Reporting to executive stakeholders
   129→- Fleet investment justification
   130→
   131→#### Project Responsibilities
   132→
   133→| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
   134→|-------|----------|------------|
   135→| Review | Validate Section 4 (Strategy) — what impresses | 4h |
   136→| Review | Validate Section 5.1.3 (Balancing) | 4h |
   137→| Review | Validate Section 7.1 (Manager Persona) | 2h |
   138→| Demo | Review script — executive language | 4h |
   139→| Demo | Participate in rehearsal as "client" | 2h |
   140→
   141→**Total estimated:** 16 hours
   142→
   143→#### Nice-to-Have
   144→
   145→- MBA or management specialization
   146→- Experience with C-level presentations
   147→- Background in finance or controlling
   148→
   149→---
   150→
   151→### 1.4 Homologation and Compliance Specialist
   152→
   153→#### Mandatory Experience
   154→
   155→| Requirement | Minimum |
   156→|-------------|---------|
   157→| Time in rolling stock homologation | 5 years |
   158→| Certification processes by country/network | Multiple countries |
   159→| TSI (Technical Specifications for Interoperability) | Detailed knowledge |
   160→| ERA (European Union Agency for Railways) | Direct interaction |
   161→
   162→#### Specific Knowledge
   163→
   164→- Aptitude matrix (train × network × service type)
   165→- Cross-border certification processes
   166→- Certificate and validity management
   167→- Configuration change impact on homologation
   168→- ERTMS/ETCS levels and compatibility
   169→- National Technical Rules (NTR) by country
   170→
   171→#### Project Responsibilities
   172→
   173→| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
   174→|-------|----------|------------|
   175→| Review | Validate Section 2.4.7 (Homologations) | 4h |
   176→| Review | Validate data model (train_aptitudes) | 2h |
   177→| Review | Validate aptitude constraints in solver | 2h |
   178→
   179→**Total estimated:** 8 hours
   180→
   181→#### Nice-to-Have
   182→
   183→- Multi-country experience (European corridors)
   184→- Knowledge of processes outside Europe
   185→- Legal/regulatory background
   186→
   187→---
   188→
   189→## 2. Technical Specialists
   190→
   191→### 2.1 Solution Architect
   192→
   193→#### Mandatory Experience
   194→
   195→| Requirement | Minimum |
   196→|-------------|---------|
   197→| Time in software architecture | 10 years |
   198→| Optimization or scheduling projects | 3+ projects |
   199→| Legacy system integration (EAM, ERP) | Proven experience |
   200→| Event-driven and microservices architectures | Mastery |
   201→

   227→**Total estimated:** 80 hours

# Professional Profiles for MMS Project

> Requirements document for hiring specialists to review the MMS Demo Playbook and work on the project.
>
> **Prepared by:** Product Owner with 15 years of experience in railway systems

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Railway Domain Specialists](#1-railway-domain-specialists)
2. [Technical Specialists](#2-technical-specialists)
3. [Product and UX Specialists](#3-product-and-ux-specialists)
4. [Quality and Delivery Specialists](#4-quality-and-delivery-specialists)
5. [Business and Presentation Specialists](#5-business-and-presentation-specialists)
6. [Summary and Prioritization](#6-summary-and-prioritization)

---

## 1. Railway Domain Specialists

### 1.1 Rolling Stock Engineer

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in rolling stock maintenance | 8 years |
| Maintenance cycles (PM1, PM2, HM, Overhaul) | Full mastery |
| Wheelset management | Reprofiling, flange limits |
| Standards | EN 15380, ECM regulation |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Maintenance intervals by train type (EMU, DMU, locomotives)
- Critical component degradation (bogie, wheelset, pantograph)
- Wayside detection systems (TBOGI, hot axle detectors)
- Fleet lifecycle and TCO calculation
- Differences between manufacturers (Alstom, Siemens, Stadler, CAF)

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 2 (Domain) — intervals, terminology | 8h |
| Review | Validate Section 9 (Seed Data) — number realism | 4h |
| Review | Complete Appendix A (Glossary) | 4h |
| Validation | Review demo before presentation | 4h |

**Total estimated:** 20 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- ECM (Entity in Charge of Maintenance) certification
- Experience with multiple manufacturers
- Work at large-scale operator (200+ trains)

---

### 1.2 Railway Operations Planner

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in railway operations planning | 10 years |
| Rolling stock diagrams | Creation and optimization |
| Crew scheduling | Integration with rolling stock |
| Disruption management | Practical experience |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Rolling stock diagram creation
- Crew constraints:
  - Route knowledge
  - Traction knowledge (certification by train type)
  - Hours of Service (legal working hour limits)
- Dead running optimization
- Depot capacity management
- Often ignored operational times:
  - Sign-on: 15-30 min
  - Walk time: 5-15 min
  - Preparation: 10-20 min
  - Securing: 5-15 min
- Shunting — time and cost

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 2.4 (Unknown Unknowns) | 8h |
| Review | Validate Section 5 (Functional Requirements) | 6h |
| Review | Validate Section 8.4 (Solver) — constraints | 8h |
| Validation | Test flows from operator perspective | 8h |
| Demo | Review script and FAQ | 6h |
| Support | Available for questions during sprints | 4h |

**Total estimated:** 40 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- Experience with DELMIA Quintiq or Tracsis
- Work at large-scale operator (500+ trains)
- Multi-modal experience (passenger + freight)

---

### 1.3 Fleet Manager

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in rolling stock fleet management | 7 years |
| Availability and utilization KPIs | Direct responsibility |
| Mileage balancing | Practical implementation |
| Maintenance contracts | TSSSA, PBC, or similar |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Fleet metrics:
  - MDBF (Mean Distance Between Failures)
  - MTTR (Mean Time To Repair)
  - Availability rate
  - Fleet utilization
- Wear balancing strategies
- Trade-offs between utilization and maintenance
- Reporting to executive stakeholders
- Fleet investment justification

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 4 (Strategy) — what impresses | 4h |
| Review | Validate Section 5.1.3 (Balancing) | 4h |
| Review | Validate Section 7.1 (Manager Persona) | 2h |
| Demo | Review script — executive language | 4h |
| Demo | Participate in rehearsal as "client" | 2h |

**Total estimated:** 16 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- MBA or management specialization
- Experience with C-level presentations
- Background in finance or controlling

---

### 1.4 Homologation and Compliance Specialist

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in rolling stock homologation | 5 years |
| Certification processes by country/network | Multiple countries |
| TSI (Technical Specifications for Interoperability) | Detailed knowledge |
| ERA (European Union Agency for Railways) | Direct interaction |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Aptitude matrix (train × network × service type)
- Cross-border certification processes
- Certificate and validity management
- Configuration change impact on homologation
- ERTMS/ETCS levels and compatibility
- National Technical Rules (NTR) by country

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 2.4.7 (Homologations) | 4h |
| Review | Validate data model (train_aptitudes) | 2h |
| Review | Validate aptitude constraints in solver | 2h |

**Total estimated:** 8 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- Multi-country experience (European corridors)
- Knowledge of processes outside Europe
- Legal/regulatory background

---

## 2. Technical Specialists

### 2.1 Solution Architect

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in software architecture | 10 years |
| Optimization or scheduling projects | 3+ projects |
| Legacy system integration (EAM, ERP) | Proven experience |
| Event-driven and microservices architectures | Mastery |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Integration patterns:
  - API (REST, GraphQL, gRPC)
  - Batch processing
  - Event streaming (Kafka, RabbitMQ)
- Architecture trade-offs:
  - Monolith vs microservices for MVP
  - When to use each approach
- Cache and synchronization strategies
- Communication between heterogeneous services (Node.js ↔ Python)
- Temporal decoupling and resilience
- Observability (logs, metrics, traces)

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 8 (Architecture) | 16h |
| Review | Validate Section 8.5 (API) | 8h |
| Review | Validate Section 8.7 (API ↔ Solver) | 8h |
| Sprint 1 | Define detailed architecture | 24h |
| Sprint 2 | Foundation code review | 16h |
| Support | Technical decisions during project | 8h |

**Total estimated:** 80 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- Cloud certification (AWS/GCP/Azure)
- Experience with mission-critical systems
- Background in transportation systems

---

### 2.2 Operations Research Specialist

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in combinatorial optimization | 5 years |
| MILP, CP-SAT, metaheuristics | Theoretical and practical mastery |
| Google OR-Tools, Gurobi, or CPLEX | Production use |
| Publications or projects in vehicle routing/scheduling | Proven |

#### Specific Knowledge

- Allocation problem modeling
- Multi-criteria objective function formulation
- Decomposition techniques:
  - Benders decomposition
  - Dantzig-Wolfe decomposition
  - Column generation
- Warm starting and solution seeding
- Explainable solution generation (not just optimal)
- Solver parameter tuning
- Infeasibility handling

#### Project Responsibilities

| Phase | Activity | Est. Hours |
|-------|----------|------------|
| Review | Validate Section 2.6 (two-stage architecture) | 8h |
| Review | Validate Section 8.4 (Solver) | 16h |
| Sprint 2 | Implement CP-SAT model | 40h |
| Sprint 3 | Justification generation | 24h |
| Sprint 4 | Tuning and performance | 24h |
| Support | Debugging and improvements | 8h |

**Total estimated:** 120 hours

#### Nice-to-Have

- PhD in Operations Research
- Specific experience with railway scheduling
- Publications in OR journals

---

### 2.3 Backend Tech Lead

#### Mandatory Experience

| Requirement | Minimum |
|-------------|---------|
| Time in backend development | 8 years |
| Time as tech lead | 3 years |
| Node.js/TypeScript | Advanced mastery |
| Python | Proficiency |
| PostgreSQL in production | Proven experience |

#### Specific Knowledge

- RESTful and gRPC API design
- Complex relational data modeling
- Cache strategies (Redis):
  - Cache-aside
  - Write-through

1→# MMS DEMO PLAYBOOK
     2→
     3→> Single reference document for creating the railway fleet management system (MMS) demo.
     4→>
     5→> **Objective:** Win the bid by impressing the client in 3 months of development.
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Table of Contents
    10→
    11→1. [Context and Objective](#1-context-and-objective)
    12→2. [Railway Domain (Discovery)](#2-railway-domain-discovery)
    13→3. [Market Analysis](#3-market-analysis)
    14→4. [Demo Strategy](#4-demo-strategy)
    15→5. [Functional Requirements](#5-functional-requirements)
    16→6. [Non-Functional Requirements](#6-non-functional-requirements)
    17→7. [UX/UI Specification](#7-uxui-specification)
    18→8. [Technical Architecture](#8-technical-architecture)
    19→9. [Data and Seed](#9-data-and-seed)
    20→10. [Timeline](#10-timeline)
    21→11. [Risks and Mitigations](#11-risks-and-mitigations)
    22→12. [Presentation Script](#12-presentation-script)
    23→13. [Appendices](#13-appendices)
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→## 1. Context and Objective
    28→
    29→### 1.1 Situation
    30→
    31→Software development company, market reference but **not specialized in railway systems**, participates in a bid to build a fleet management system (MMS - Maintenance Management System).
    32→
    33→### 1.2 Objective
    34→
    35→Create functional demo in **3 months** that:
    36→- Demonstrates technical competence
    37→- Proves understanding of the railway domain
    38→- Impresses decision-makers (Operations Director, client's CTO)
    39→
    40→### 1.3 Success Metric
    41→
    42→> After 15 minutes of demo, the client thinks: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**
    43→
    44→### 1.4 What This Project Is NOT
    45→
    46→| Is Not | Justification |
    47→|--------|---------------|
    48→| Production system | Demo for bidding, not real operation |
    49→| Complete MVP | Focus on impressing, not feature completeness |
    50→| Real EAM integration | Mocked data is sufficient |
    51→| Solution for all problems | Solving 1 pain point well > 10 pain points poorly |
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→## 2. Railway Domain (Discovery)
    56→
    57→> **MANDATORY READING** for all team members before starting any work.
    58→
    59→### 2.1 Essential Glossary
    60→
    61→| Term | Definition | System Use |
    62→|------|------------|------------|
    63→| **Rolling Stock** | Trains, locomotives, wagons | Main managed entity |
    64→| **Consist** | Composition of units forming a train | A "train" can have multiple units |
    65→| **Married Pair** | Mechanically inseparable units | Cannot be allocated separately |
    66→| **Dead Running** | Movement without passengers/cargo | Cost to minimize |
    67→| **Shunting** | Train maneuvering in yard/depot | Consumes 15-30 min + cost |
    68→| **LMD** | Light Maintenance Depot | Light maintenance (PM1, PM2) |
    69→| **HMD** | Heavy Maintenance Depot | Heavy maintenance (overhaul) |
    70→| **PM** | Preventive Maintenance | Scheduled preventive maintenance |
    71→| **HM** | Heavy Maintenance | Heavy maintenance |
    72→| **Wheelset** | Wheel + axle assembly | Most critical component for balancing |
    73→| **Bogie** | Structure with wheels under car | Highest MTTR, failure point |
    74→| **Timetable** | Fixed schedule of services | Already exists, MMS doesn't create |
    75→| **Service/Run** | A specific route in the timetable | MMS allocates train to service |
    76→| **Aptitude/Homologation** | Train certification for route/country | Hard allocation constraint |
    77→
    78→### 2.2 Business Entities
    79→
    80→```
    81→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    82→│                        CONCEPTUAL MODEL                          │
    83→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    84→│                                                                 │
    85→│  ┌─────────┐ belongs to  ┌─────────┐  has       ┌────────────┐  │
    86→│  │  TRAIN  │────────────▶│  DEPOT  │◀──────────│  CAPACITY  │  │
    87→│  └────┬────┘             └─────────┘           └────────────┘  │
    88→│       │                                                         │
    89→│       │ executes                                                │
    90→│       ▼                                                         │
    91→│  ┌─────────┐  part of    ┌───────────┐                         │
    92→│  │ SERVICE │────────────▶│ TIMETABLE │                         │
    93→│  └────┬────┘             └───────────┘                         │
    94→│       │                                                         │
    95→│       │ accumulates                                             │
    96→│       ▼                                                         │
    97→│  ┌─────────┐  triggers   ┌─────────────┐  occurs at            │
    98→│  │MILEAGE  │────────────▶│ MAINTENANCE │───────────▶ DEPOT     │
    99→│  └─────────┘             └─────────────┘                        │
   100→│                                                                 │
   101→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   102→```
   103→
   104→#### Critical Relationships
   105→
   106→| Relationship | Cardinality | Business Rule |
   107→|--------------|-------------|---------------|
   108→| Train → Depot | N:1 | Train has "home" depot |
   109→| Train → Service | N:M | One train executes various services; service can have different trains on different days |
   110→| Train → Maintenance | 1:N | Train has maintenance history |
   111→| Depot → Capacity | 1:1 | Each depot has limited capacity |
   112→| Service → Timetable | N:1 | Services belong to a timetable |
   113→
   114→### 2.3 Train Lifecycle
   115→
   116→```
   117→    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   118→    │                    OPERATIONAL CYCLE                     │
   119→    │                                                          │
   120→    │   ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐             │
   121→    │   │  DEPOT  │───▶│ SERVICE │───▶│  DEPOT  │             │
   122→    │   │ (start) │    │  (run)  │    │  (end)  │             │
   123→    │   └─────────┘    └─────────┘    └────┬────┘             │
   124→    │                                      │                   │
   125→    │        ┌─────────────────────────────┘                   │
   126→    │        │                                                 │
   127→    │        ▼                                                 │
   128→    │   ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐    ┌─────────┐             │
   129→    │   │  CHECK  │───▶│  MAINT  │───▶│ RETURN  │             │
   130→    │   │ (need?) │    │  (fix)  │    │ (ready) │             │
   131→    │   └─────────┘    └─────────┘    └─────────┘             │
   132→    │                                                          │
   133→    └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   134→```
   135→
   136→#### Typical Maintenance Intervals
   137→
   138→| Level | Interval | Duration | Lead Time | Location |
   139→|-------|----------|----------|-----------|----------|
   140→| PM1 | 10,000-30,000 km | 4-8 hours | 1-2 weeks | LMD |
   141→| PM2 | 60,000-120,000 km | 1-3 days | 2-4 weeks | LMD |
   142→| HM | 6-12 years | 2-6 weeks | 6-12 months | HMD |
   143→| Overhaul | 12-20 years | 2-6 months | 12-24 months | HMD |
   144→
   145→### 2.4 Unknown Unknowns (Critical Discoveries)
   146→
   147→> Items that are not obvious but, if ignored, can invalidate the system.
   148→
   149→#### 2.4.1 Wheelset
   150→
   151→| Fact | Implication |
   152→|------|-------------|
   153→| Flange < 28.5mm = mandatory reprofiling | Hard constraint |
   154→| Wear is **non-linear** | Balancing only total km is insufficient |
   155→| Curves and climate accelerate degradation | Must weight by route profile |
   156→| Typical interval: ~60,000 km | More frequent than PM2 |
   157→
   158→#### 2.4.2 Bogie
   159→
   160→| Fact | Implication |
   161→|------|-------------|
   162→| Components in series | Single point of failure |
   163→| MTTR: 4-72 hours | Highest impact on availability |
   164→| Wayside systems (TBOGI) detect degradation | If client has it, integrate for prediction |
   165→
   166→#### 2.4.3 Consist (Composition)
   167→
   168→| Fact | Implication |
   169→|------|-------------|
   170→| Trains are compositions (A+B) | Don't treat "train" as atom |
   171→| Married pairs are inseparable | Model must track integrity |
   172→| Coupling/decoupling has cost | Consider in optimization |
   173→
   174→#### 2.4.4 Train Identification
   175→
   176→| Fact | Implication |
   177→|------|-------------|
   178→| Operational ID changes at borders | Separate `physical_asset_id` from `operational_number` |
   179→| UIC number is European standard | Use as canonical ID if applicable |
   180→
   181→#### 2.4.5 Crew
   182→
   183→| Fact | Implication |
   184→|------|-------------|
   185→| Train allocation is interdependent with crew | "Perfect" plan fails without driver |
   186→| Route knowledge: driver only operates known routes | Qualification constraint |
   187→| Traction knowledge: certification by train type | Another constraint |
   188→| Hours of Service: legal limits | Temporal constraint |
   189→| Ignored operational times: sign-on (15-30min), walk (5-15min), preparation (10-20min), securing (5-15min) | Adds 30-60 min/service |
   190→
   191→**For Demo:** Model crew as simplified constraint (availability per depot/shift). Complete integration in future phase.
   192→
   193→#### 2.4.6 Shunting
   194→
   195→| Fact | Implication |
   196→|------|-------------|
   197→| Moving train within depot: 15-30 min | Plans that ignore this become unworkable |
   198→| Cost: ~€350/maneuver | Must minimize |
   199→
   200→#### 2.4.7 Homologations
   201→
   202→| Fact | Implication |
   203→|------|-------------|
   204→| Train certified by country/network | Aptitude is matrix (train × network × service type) |
   205→| Not a simple boolean | Data model must support |
   206→
   207→#### 2.4.8 Infrastructure Windows
   208→
   209→| Fact | Implication |
   210→|------|-------------|
   211→| Tracks have their own maintenance | Route may be blocked even with train available |
   212→| Usually informed in advance | Integrate as route constraint |
   213→
   214→### 2.5 What the System Does NOT Do
   215→
   216→| Responsibility | Who Defines | MMS Only... |
   217→|----------------|-------------|-------------|
   218→| Maintenance intervals | Manufacturer / Regulator | Optimizes WITHIN the window |
   219→| Timetable | Transportation Planning | Allocates train to existing service |
   220→| Routes | Infrastructure | Uses routes as input |
   221→| Depot capacity | Engineering | Respects as constraint |
   222→
   223→### 2.6 Correct Mental Model
   224→
   225→```
   226→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   227→│                     DECISION HIERARCHY                           │
   228→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   229→│                                                                 │
   230→│  STRATEGIC (months-years)          OPERATIONAL (days-weeks)    │
   231→│  ┌─────────────────────┐           ┌─────────────────────┐     │
   232→│  │ When each train     │           │ Which train executes│     │
   233→│  │ enters maintenance  │──────────▶│ which service today │     │
   234→│  │ (macro schedule)    │ constrains│ (daily allocation)  │     │
   235→│  └─────────────────────┘           └─────────────────────┘     │
   236→│           │                                   │                 │
   237→│           │ feedback if                       │                 │
   238→│           │ infeasible                        │                 │
   239→│           └───────────────────────────────────┘                 │
   240→│                                                                 │
   241→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   242→```
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## 3. Market Analysis
   247→
   248→### 3.1 Main Competitors
   249→
   250→#### Tier 1 — Enterprise Rail
   251→
   252→| System | Company | Strengths | Weaknesses |
   253→|--------|---------|-----------|------------|
   254→| **DELMIA Quintiq** | Dassault | Market leader, end-to-end solution, advanced optimization | Very high cost, 12-24 month implementation, lock-in |
   255→| **zedas®asset** | ZEDAS | ECM compliance specialist, strong in Germany | Less flexible, compliance focus over optimization |
   256→| **Railfleet** | Railnova | Modern UX, telemetry and IoT focus | Doesn't focus on allocation optimization |
   257→| **TRACS-RS** | Tracsis | Rolling stock diagrams, strong in UK | Regional, less international |
   258→
   259→#### Tier 2 — Generic EAM
   260→
   261→| System | Company | Strengths | Weaknesses |
   262→|--------|---------|-----------|------------|
   263→| **Maximo for Transportation** | IBM | Mature, enterprise-grade | Generic, requires heavy customization |
   264→| **SAP PM / S/4HANA** | SAP | SAP ecosystem integration | Not rail-specific |
   265→
   266→### 3.2 Market Gap
   267→
   268→| Observation | Opportunity |
   269→|-------------|-------------|
   270→| No open-source solution for rail | Differentiation if considering open model |
   271→| Km balancing not a primary feature | Can be a differentiator |
   272→| Generally dated UX | Modern interface impresses |
   273→| Weak Maintenance ↔ Operations integration | Unified interface is a differentiator |
   274→
   275→### 3.3 Market Visual Patterns
   276→
   277→Common elements in reference systems:
   278→
   279→| Element | Use | Example |
   280→|---------|-----|---------|
   281→| Traffic-light status | Quick state (green/yellow/red) | Train health, depot occupancy |
   282→| Depot cards | Cards with occupancy gauge | Main dashboard |
   283→| Interactive Gantt | Maintenance timeline with drag-drop | Planning |
   284→| Map with clustering | Train positions, zoom groups | Operational view |
   285→| Alert feed | Real-time list | Sidebar or modal |
   286→| Drill-down | Fleet → Train → Component → History | Standard navigation |
   287→
   288→### 3.4 Proposed Differentiator for MMS
   289→
   290→| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
   291→|----------------|----------------|
   292→| Km balancing as primary objective | Competitors treat as secondary |
   293→| Unified Maintenance ↔ Operations interface | Eliminates silos, reduces rework |
   294→| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit |
   295→| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" |
   296→| Visible decision justification | Trust, not black box |
   297→
   298→---
   299→
   300→## 4. Demo Strategy
   301→
   302→### 4.1 Fundamental Principle
   303→
   304→> **Depth > Breadth**
   305→>
   306→> Solving ONE pain point very well impresses more than 20 superficial features.
   307→
   308→**Chosen pain point:** Fleet mileage balancing.
   309→
   310→- Visually clear (comparative charts)
   311→- Obvious financial impact (lifecycle, TCO)
   312→- Competitive differentiator (competitors don't focus)
   313→
   314→### 4.2 Wow Moments (What Stays in Memory)
   315→
   316→| Moment | When | Effect on Client |
   317→|--------|------|------------------|
   318→| Map with "live" trains | Demo start | "They understand our operation" |
   319→| Interactive simulation | Demo middle | Client feels ownership, interacts |
   320→| Decision justification | Demo end | "Not a black box, I can trust" |
   321→| Correct terminology | Throughout demo | "They know our business" |
   322→
   323→### 4.3 Credibility Checklist
   324→
   325→| Item | Status | Responsible |
   326→|------|--------|-------------|
   327→| Glossary mastered by team | ☐ | Everyone |
   328→| Seed data with plausible names | ☐ | Data dev |
   329→| Realistic km and intervals | ☐ | Data dev |
   330→| Correct terminology in UI | ☐ | Designer |
   331→| Domain FAQ prepared | ☐ | PO |
   332→| Domain consultant consulted | ☐ | PO |
   333→
   334→### 4.4 What to Avoid (Anti-patterns)
   335→
   336→| Anti-pattern | Why It's Fatal | Mitigation |
   337→|--------------|----------------|------------|
   338→| Generic UI | "Looks like any system" | Rail-specific elements |
   339→| Obviously fake data | "Train 1, Train 2, Depot A" | Realistic names and numbers |
   340→| Black box | "Why did it recommend this?" | Always visible justification |
   341→| Too many shallow features | "Doesn't really work" | Focus on 1 complete flow |
   342→| Slow loading | "System doesn't scale" | Optimize for demo |

   344→
   345→### 4.5 Scope IN (Do)
   346→
   347→| Feature | Priority | Justification |
   348→|---------|----------|---------------|
   349→| Dashboard with KPIs | Must | First impression |
   350→| Map with train positions | Must | Visual wow |
   351→| Km balancing visualization | Must | Main pain point |
   352→| Working allocation engine | Must | Proof of technical capability |
   353→| Scenario simulation | Must | Client interaction |
   354→| Recommendation justification | Must | Trust |
   355→| Maintenance Gantt | Should | Market expectation |
   356→| Visual alerts | Should | Demonstrates proactivity |

   358→
   359→### 4.6 Scope OUT (Don't Do)
   360→
   361→| Feature | Why Out | When to Include |
   362→|---------|---------|-----------------|
   363→| MILP optimization (long-term) | Complexity, 3 months insufficient | Phase 2 |
   364→| Real EAM integration | Depends on client environment | Phase 2 |
   365→| Disruption management | Different architecture, huge scope | Phase 3 |

   372→## 5. Functional Requirements
   373→
   374→### 5.1 Modules and Features
   375→
   376→#### 5.1.1 Dashboard (Must Have)
   377→
   378→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   379→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   380→| Fleet KPIs | Total trains, available, in maintenance, % utilization | Numbers updated in < 5s |
   381→| Depot occupancy | Gauge per depot showing current vs capacity | Speedometer-type visual |
   382→| Active alerts | List of critical alerts (over/under-running, upcoming maintenance) | Sorted by criticality |
   383→| Km variance | Fleet km distribution indicator | Green (< 10%), yellow (10-20%), red (> 20%) |
   384→
   385→#### 5.1.2 Map (Must Have)
   386→
   387→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   388→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   389→| Train positions | Icons on map showing location | Can be last known position |
   390→| Depots | Depot markers with occupancy | Click opens details |
   391→| Routes | Lines connecting points | Simplified for demo |
   392→| Clustering | Grouping on zoom out | Number indicates quantity |
   393→

   395→
   396→#### 5.1.3 Km Balancing (Must Have)
   397→
   398→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   399→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   400→| Distribution chart | Histogram or box plot of fleet | Shows mean, outliers |
   401→| Train ranking | List sorted by km (over/under-running) | Top 10 each extreme |
   402→| Trend | Temporal chart showing evolution | Last 6 months |
   403→| Drill-down | Click on train opens details | Km and service history |
   404→

   406→
   407→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   408→|---------|-------------|---------------------|

   412→| Apply allocation | Confirm choice | Updates state |
   413→
   414→**Justification Example:**
   415→```
   416→Recommendation: Train T-1234 for Service S-789
   417→
   418→Reasons:
   419→• Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)
   420→• Service ends 5km from maintenance depot
   421→• PM scheduled in 3 days, destination depot has available slot
   422→• Aptitude confirmed for Lisbon-Porto route
   423→
   424→Score: 94/100
   425→```
   426→
   427→#### 5.1.5 Simulation / What-If (Must Have)
   428→
   429→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   430→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   431→| Create scenario | Duplicate current state for sandbox | Isolated scenario |
   432→| Modify parameters | Change allocations, maintenance dates | Drag-drop or form interface |
   433→| Compare results | Side-by-side KPIs | Comparison table |
   434→| Projection | Show impact in 30/60/90 days | Trend charts |
   435→
   436→#### 5.1.6 Maintenance Gantt (Should Have)
   437→
   438→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   439→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   440→| Visual timeline | Bars per train showing maintenances | Horizontal scroll |
   441→| Drag-drop | Move maintenance to another date | Updates and validates conflicts |
   442→| Colors by type | PM1, PM2, HM differentiated | Visible legend |
   443→| Conflicts | Visual highlight if depot full | Red border |
   444→
   445→#### 5.1.7 Alerts (Should Have)
   446→
   447→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   448→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   449→| Real-time feed | Updating alert list | WebSocket or 30s polling |
   450→| Categories | Over-running, under-running, maintenance, capacity | Filter by type |
   451→| Quick action | Link to resolve (go to train, allocation) | 1 click |
   452→
   453→#### 5.1.8 Export (Could Have)
   454→
   455→| Feature | Description | Acceptance Criteria |
   456→|---------|-------------|---------------------|
   457→| PDF | Fleet status report | Professional layout |
   458→| Excel | Km and maintenance data | Organized columns |
   459→
   460→### 5.2 Main User Stories
   461→
   462→```
   463→US-01: As a Planner, I want to visualize fleet km distribution
   464→       to identify over/under-running trains.
   465→
   466→US-02: As a Planner, I want to receive a recommendation for which train to allocate
   467→       to a service, with visible justification.
   468→
   469→US-03: As a Planner, I want to simulate allocation scenarios to compare
   470→       impact before deciding.
   471→
   472→US-04: As a Dispatcher, I want to see current train positions on the map
   473→       for quick decisions.
   474→
   475→US-05: As a Manager, I want a dashboard with KPIs for executive view
   476→       without needing to navigate multiple screens.
   477→
   478→US-06: As a Planner, I want to see maintenance timeline in Gantt
   479→       to identify capacity conflicts.
   480→```

   482→---
   483→
   484→## 6. Non-Functional Requirements
   485→
   486→### 6.1 Performance
   487→

   489→|-------------|--------|---------------|

   491→| Screen loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility |
